title: Add cos-to-true / margin / mean-rank to evaluate() in both perception runners
description: In TrainNice.evaluate + frozen_head eval, compute per-trial continuous metrics from the embeddings + concept bank already in hand: (1) cos_to_true = cos(pred, true_concept_vec), report mean+std; (2) margin = cos_to_true - mean(cos to other 199), mean+std; (3) mean rank of true concept (1..200). Add to the result dict + log to mlflow (Tracking.metrics). Also log mean cos-to-true on the VAL set per epoch = eval-side mirror of the InfoNCE loss (consistency check). Accuracy metrics stay unchanged.
close_reason: Added Nice.retrieval_continuous (shared, one home in nice.py): cos_to_true mean/std, margin (cos_true - mean cos-to-others) mean/std, mean_rank of true concept. Normalizes both operands so it's true cosine regardless of caller scale. Wired into both eval methods (TrainNice.evaluate + frozen_head._retrieval) -> result dict + mlflow test_* metrics, plus per-epoch val_cos_to_true (eval-side mirror of InfoNCE loss). Unit-tested (perfect/chance/scale-invariant). Verified end-to-end: NICE 2ep smoke cos climbs 0.062->0.084 as val-top1 climbs (tracks loss); frozen mean_lin chance arm cos 0.013/margin 0.001 (~zero, matches chance). Accuracy metrics unchanged.

title: (EPIC) Continuous perception eval metrics — surface the angular error distribution alongside retrieval accuracy
description: Retrieval top-k accuracy collapses the ranked 200-way list to one bit (did #1 hit), discarding geometry: a rank-2 miss at 5deg and a rank-180 miss at 120deg score identically. Add CONTINUOUS eval metrics (cos-to-true mean/std, discrimination margin, mean-rank-of-true) ALONGSIDE the accuracy — never replacing it (accuracy stays the field-comparable number). These expose the real error distribution + let a method show it tightened the spread even when top-1 didn't move. Cheap: evaluate() already holds the trial embeddings + 200-vector concept bank. Loss connection: InfoNCE already optimizes cos-to-true + margin, so the eval metric mirrors the loss — a built-in consistency check.
close_reason: Backfilled continuous metrics across the frozen CBraMod head zoo (mean/flat/pos_attn/topo_g24/gcn, 120ep off disk cache) + readout in learning/2026-07-14_perception_continuous_metrics.md. Findings: every arm ~orthogonal to true concept (cos 0.001-0.058 = arccos ~86-90deg, incl best topo 1.75%); raw cos/z geometry-confounded (all z -3.0..-3.6, EEG embeddings sit outside the tight CLIP concept cluster - random-pair cos 0.334); MARGIN + MEAN_RANK track accuracy (mean_rank 97->70, margin 0.001->0.017), raw cos does not; retrieval lives in a tiny positive margin. Higher accuracy != tighter angular error (cos_std ~0.02-0.03 flat). NICE/ft ride their next train (no dedicated retrain). Epic 2y7k complete: metric (.1) + interpretable reference (.2) + backfill/readout (.3).

title: Grow EEGPT temporal resolution via overlapping patch_stride (N=4 -> ~N=13)
description: At N=4 the temporal sequence is too short for any module (GRU/TCN/conv all ~equivalent; temporal_cnn gave a faint 0.77). Grow N by overlapping patches: EEGTransformer patch_stride < patch_size. stride 16 on 1s@256 -> N=(256-64)//16+1=13. Register a second backbone 'eegpt_ov' (stride 16) so the cache is keyed separately; RECOMPUTE the feature cache (~4min GPU) then run the temporal-head sweep. If the faint 0.77 amplifies with more time tokens -> temporal resolution is the lever + GRU/TCN become worth trying; if still chance -> EEGPT frozen features lack transferable perception signal (honest negative). CAVEAT: EEGPT didn't pretrain with overlapping patches, so frozen features may drift - documented assumption to weigh against the result.
close_reason: grow-N at fair 50% overlap (stride32, N=7) = chance across all arms: mean 0.57 / flat 0.60 / temporal 0.60 single-trial top1 (chance 0.5). Temporal's only >chance point (0.78 at N=4 with 75% OOD overlap) collapses to chance at honest 50% overlap = it was an overlap-OOD artifact, not signal. EEGPT frozen negative CONFIRMED at fair overlap. Grow-N is not a lever. Frozen ceiling stays topo_cnn 1.78 (CBraMod).

title: Temporal head keeps EEGPT summary tokens (16-token conv, not summary-mean)
description: The temporal_cnn head means EEGPT's embed_num=4 summary tokens away before the temporal conv (mean over dim=2 -> [B,N=4,d]), giving 0.77 (~chance). Keep them: conv over the full N*embed_num=16 ordered token sequence instead. Free (rides the existing eegpt cache, ~1min). Tests whether the head was starving on info before reaching for grow-N. Compare vs 0.77.
close_reason: keep-summary temporal head (conv over full 16-token sequence, not summary-mean): 0.78 vs summary-mean 0.77 = NO change (null). Head wasn't starving on summary info; at N=4 the head isn't the bottleneck.

title: Model = Backbone + Head composite — unify the encoder zoo (good architecture)
description:
Owner ask: a general Model class with backbone + head FIELDS, a composite of what the perception work already does implicitly. Today the encoder zoo is fused/ad-hoc: NiceEncoder (monolithic eeg->embed), CBraModEncoder (backbone + _pool + head fused in one Module), frozen_head.Head (tokens->embed on cached feats). All are the SAME shape: a feature extractor + a projection to CLIP space. Unify (owner: strategy pattern / maximum unification):

CONTRACT: Backbone: [B,C,T] raw eeg -> [B,n_tok,d] token grid (CBraMod/EEGPT/NICE-stem; carries geometry: patch_points/sample_rate/d + electrode pos). Head: [B,n_tok,d] -> [B,embed] L2-normed CLIP embedding (mean/flat/pos_attn/topo/gcn/mlp). Model(backbone, head, freeze_backbone): nn.Module, forward = head(backbone(x)) normalized -> satisfies the existing ImageEncoder contract so it plugs into the registry + train_nice unchanged. encode_tokens(x)=backbone(x) is the frozen-cache hook (cache backbone output, train head alone).

This makes: frozen-probe = Model(frozen bb, head); ft = Model(unfrozen bb, head); backbone swap (m69x.3 EEGPT) = new Backbone, same head zoo — no bespoke encoder. LoadedBackbone (m69x.1) folds into Backbone. INCREMENTAL: build composite.py + wrap CBraMod as reference Backbone first (satisfies contract, registry-compatible), migrate frozen_head Head zoo into Head subclasses, then optionally express NICE as Model(NiceBackbone, ProjHead). Keep NICE working throughout (it already satisfies eeg->normed-embed). Gates: fitness/ast-grep/import-linter must stay green; test-mirror for the new logic module.
close_reason: Model=Backbone+Head composite delivered (composite.py: Backbone/Head/Model + TokenHead/PosAttn/Topo/Gcn zoo). CBraModEncoder->CBraModBackbone+Model composites (cbramod/ft/ft_attn). frozen_head migrated to composite Heads (dup zoo removed), equivalence verified (5 arms reproduce exactly). Fixed a real bug the migration surfaced: flat pool lazy-init left its MLP out of the optimizer (0.35 vs 1.25) -> size at construction from n_tok, regression-tested. Wired perception into the existing mlflow (per-epoch + system GPU metrics). test_foundation unchanged+green, 16 tests, all gates green.

title: Integrate a 2nd frozen backbone targeting the S=1 handicap; compare frozen ceilings
description:
The real frozen-loop lever. CBraMod on 1s/200Hz = S=1 (single 200pt patch) -> sub-patch ERP timing unreachable by any head. Research + wire >=1 alternate frozen EEG backbone that either (a) tokenizes 1s into S>1 finer temporal patches, or (b) has a less-clinical pretraining domain, so its FROZEN features carry perception structure CBraMod's don't. Emit [B,C,S,d] via the task-1 backbone registry, run the EXISTING 5-head sweep (mean/flat/pos_attn/topo/gcn) on it, compare best-head frozen top1 vs CBraMod's 1.68. Candidates to scope in a research pass: LaBraM (needs a 10-20->63ch projection adapter, license check), EEGPT, BENDR, Neuro-GPT — pick by patch granularity on a 1s epoch + license.

WALL-TIME: research/selection ~delegate (Haiku researcher, ~10min, no GPU); wiring+adapter ~code; first precompute+sweep ~8-12min GPU per backbone (precompute dominates, then head sweep off cache). Acceptance: >=1 alternate backbone wired + its frozen ceiling reported next to CBraMod's. A backbone whose frozen ceiling clears 1.68 by >=0.1 = the lever found; if it ties/loses, the frozen ceiling is a clinical-pretraining property (honest negative, documented). Depends on task 1.
notes: Temporal head result (2026-07-14, off eegpt cache): mean 0.63 / flat 0.53 / temporal_cnn 0.77 (chance 0.5). Temporal (1D conv along N=4 time-patches, order-preserving) BEATS the order-blind mean (0.63->0.77) = a FAINT real temporal signal, but still ~chance, best_val@ep1 (no real learning), far from CBraMod 1.78. Read: at N=4 the sequence is too SHORT for the module to matter - GRU/TCN/conv all ~equivalent over 4 steps, Mamba pointless (nothing to select). The faint lift says signal EXISTS but needs resolution. NEXT (owner discussion - GRU/TCN expected, grow-N gated on GPU): (1) grow N via overlapping patch_stride (16 -> N=15 on 1s@256) = cache RECOMPUTE ~4min GPU, THEN a temporal module has a real sequence; (2) keep the embed_num=4 summary tokens (currently mean-pooled) = free; (3) only THEN GRU/TCN differ. NOT a negative - a live investigation: the first mean/flat run was the wrong head, temporal-conv shows the faint signal, grow-N is the prerequisite to amplify it.
close_reason: EEGPT frozen backbone = REAL negative (multi-condition, not the initial wrong-head fluke). Frozen ~chance across: 3 heads (mean 0.63/flat 0.53/temporal 0.78) x 2 temporal-res (N=4, N=13-overlap all <=0.78) x summary-mean/keep (0.77/0.78). vs CBraMod frozen ceiling 1.78. Temporal-resolution hypothesis NOT supported: S=4/S=13 tokens don't help because EEGPT frozen features (clinical/BCI pretrain + channel-fusion into summary tokens) lack zero-shot-transferable perception structure. GRU/TCN/Mamba moot - no signal to model. CAVEATS on record: overlap OOD, exact input-scaling untested (val>chance says features live-ish), GRU/TCN untested (equivalent at N=4, grow-N also chance). Frozen ceiling STAYS 1.78 (CBraMod topo-g24 geometry). Verdict: frozen HEAD (grid res) moved the number; frozen BACKBONE swap did not. Next >1.78 = adaptation (LoRA/ft 29z), out of frozen-first scope.

title: Disk feature-cache + backbone-parameterized precompute (frozen-loop substrate)
description:
Enabling refactor for the whole frozen-lever epic. Today frozen_head._features caches the [N,C·S,d] frozen grid in RAM ONLY (recomputed every run) and _build_cache hardwires Foundation._load_backbone() (CBraMod). Two changes: (1) PERSIST the feature cache to disk keyed by (backbone, train-set, split) -> a head/backbone sweep skips the backbone forward entirely (seconds, not minutes); (2) parameterize the backbone (a --backbone flag + a small backbone registry emitting [B,C,S,d]), so a new frozen backbone is a one-line add, not a fork. Keep the montage/pos plumbing backbone-agnostic. This is what makes tasks 2+3 cheap.

WALL-TIME: pure code + gates (~dev, no experiment). One-time GPU precompute per (backbone,split) ~2-4min (267k train + 16k test epochs through the backbone); every reuse instant (disk load). Acceptance: second run on the same backbone loads cache in seconds (no backbone forward); --backbone selects the loader; existing 5-head CBraMod result reproduces off the cache.
close_reason: Disk feature-cache + --backbone seam shipped + verified. Foundation.load_backbone(name)->LoadedBackbone registry (cbramod entry); frozen_head persists float16 token grid + concept/files to <data>/cache keyed by (backbone,split,subjects), backbone loaded only on miss. Cold 3m1s -> warm 52s (cache hits, no forward), 5-head numbers reproduce. Lint+foundation-test green.

title: Frozen backbone->head as the primary perception lever — map & lift the frozen ceiling
description:
Owner strategy: get a reasonably good signal from FROZEN backbone -> head FIRST; LoRA/full-ft are optimization ABOVE that, other backbones help too. Rationale: frozen->head is the CHEAP DECOUPLED loop (cache backbone features once, train tiny heads in seconds) and it prices adaptation honestly — LoRA/ft only earn their GPU if they clear the frozen ceiling by >=0.1.

STATE (from nm5/j4l head search): CBraMod-frozen->head is ~MAPPED. Geometry is the lever (spatial preservation), topo_cnn (RBF scalp-grid + 2D CNN) = frozen ceiling ~1.68 single-trial top1 (beats NICE 1.6 with ZERO backbone training); pooling/depth dead; GCN worse (1.07); 300ep HURTS (overfits, 1.57<=1.68@120). So MORE HEAD VARIANTS won't clear 0.1 — that axis is closed. Full-ft unlocks 2.3 (clinical TUEG frozen features are off-domain; only adaptation cashes the rest).

The real frozen-loop headroom is the BACKBONE: CBraMod on a 1s/200Hz stimulus = S=1 (one single 200pt patch), so sub-patch ERP timing is compressed in one frozen rep, unreachable by ANY head. A backbone with finer temporal patching (S>1 on 1s) or a less clinical pretraining domain could lift the frozen CEILING — the one move no head can make.

SCOPE: (1) persist feature cache to disk + parameterize the backbone (substrate — makes every sweep seconds); (2) close the head axis honestly (topo grid/sigma mini-sweep, never tuned); (3) swap in >=1 alternate frozen backbone targeting the S=1 handicap, compare frozen ceilings. OUT OF SCOPE: LoRA (29z), full-ft — optimization above. HONESTY: report frozen ceiling per backbone even if <ft 2.3; NO multi-seed until a backbone moves the ceiling >=0.1 (stage-gated rigor).
close_reason: Epic frozen-first scope EXHAUSTED with documented verdict (7/7 children done). Frozen HEAD ceiling = topo_cnn ~1.78 single-trial top1 (grid/sigma sweep, beats NICE 1.6 with zero backbone training); pooling/depth/GCN dead; head axis closed. Frozen BACKBONE swap (EEGPT) = real multi-condition negative (all chance at fair overlap) — clinical/BCI frozen features lack zero-shot perception structure, S=1 handicap unfixable frozen. Infra landed: disk feature-cache + Backbone+Head composite + mlflow. Next >1.78 = ADAPTATION (LoRA/full-ft, out of frozen scope) -> 07m/29z.

title: Per-subject EEG alignment before the encoder (Stage-1/2 transfer template)
description: Highest-prior transfer lever. Stages 1-2 closed their cross-subject gaps by removing per-SUBJECT distribution displacement (MI: Riemannian re-centering; workload: per-subject z-score). Stage-3 encoder has per-CHANNEL global z-score only, not per-SUBJECT. Add per-subject alignment (subtract/scale by each subject's own EEG feature stats, unsupervised) before InfoNCE. CHEAP test: single train-4/test-5 run, does cross-subject single-trial top1 move above ~1.9%? Keep/kill.
notes: Investigated the WHY (not a bare kill): val-up/test-down signature => residual-rotation mechanism, not structure-destruction. Whitening leaves per-subject rotation the conv encoder can't handle zero-shot (RPA step-3 problem). Grounds the escalation to 36g (embedding-space invariance, rotation-agnostic). See bd memory perception-signal-recenter-fails.
close_reason: KILL. Per-subject signal re-centering does not improve cross-subject retrieval (matched: single 1.71=1.71, concept 3.0<5.5). Raw-signal whitening destroys shared spatial structure; MI covariance-feature template doesn't port. Code kept opt-in+documented. See bd memory perception-signal-recenter-fails.

title: [PHASE 1] Close Stage-3 perception cross-subject transfer (method iteration)
description: Stage-3 perception (EEG->image) has BAD cross-subject transfer (single-trial retrieval ~1.9%, ~4x chance, near-floor). OBJECTIVE: CLOSE the transfer gap via fast METHOD iteration — not measure it. Loop: mechanism-driven method tweak -> cheapest single-run test that shows signal -> keep/kill -> next. Highest-prior levers (transplanted from Stages 1-2 where they worked): (1) per-SUBJECT alignment/normalization of EEG before the encoder (the analog of Stage-2 per-subject z-score / Stage-1 Riemannian re-centering); (2) domain-adversarial subject-invariance (gradient reversal on subject id); (3) concept-aware InfoNCE (kill same-concept false negatives). NO sweeps/ablations/multi-seed until a method actually MOVES the number (see bd memory early-stage-skip-rigor). JUDGMENT GATE: did any tweak lift cross-subject single-trial top1 meaningfully above ~1.9%? Keep winners, document kills.
acceptance_criteria: train-9 LOSO run recorded; xam parity multi-seeded (mean+/-std); 3v6 cross-subject+calibration number reported; 8r2+59q closed; READMEs updated with the finalized perception numbers; sync_numbers 0-stale; tests green.
notes: RE-SCOPED 2026-07-09: was 'subject-count headline sweep + multi-seed parity' = premature rigor on a broken method (owner correctly rejected). Now: base-transfer method iteration. e30 (subject-count sweep) + 9s5 (multi-seed parity) closed as not-the-objective.
close_reason: PHASE 1 COMPLETE — judgment gate crossed with an honest NEGATIVE. Members done: 8r2/8hl/3v6/59q (honest retrieval audit, cross-subject gap+calibration, cross-dataset null, EEG1 adapter); e30/9s5 (subject-count sweep + multi-seed — closed as not-the-objective after re-scope); dpi/36g/lbd (the 3 transfer levers — input re-centering, domain-adversarial, concept-aware soft-InfoNCE — ALL fail to lift single-trial cross-subject retrieval beyond noise); sr3 (consolidation). GATE OUTCOME: no lever closes the gap -> the ~1.9% single-trial cross-subject floor is SNR/capacity-bound, robust to transfer/loss interventions across 3 axes. Documented in visual/README + bd memory perception-cross-subject-hard-floor. A clean publishable negative. Remaining untested axis = encoder capacity (different question).

title: Run train-9 LOSO on THINGS-EEG2 — the headline subject-count number
description: The real cross-subject lever is SUBJECT COUNT (train-1 2% -> train-4 6% concept-avg top1). visual/README promises the full train-9 LOSO 'one run away' but it's untracked. Run train[1-9]/test-10 (representative fold) with the optimized config (balanced+CLIP-hard+hard_beta+bf16+train_frac) for speed; report concept-avg + single-trial top1/5 vs the train-4 point. GPU. This is the number the perception headline is waiting on.
notes: First train-9 run (optimized cfg, frac0.2, 40ep) UNDERTRAINED: best_val_epoch=39=cap, val-top1 monotonic 1.27->3.42% (not converged). concept_avg top1 4.5% / single 2.26% — but confounded vs README train-4 (diff test subj + diff config). Re-running as clean subject-count sweep {1,4,9}/test-10, converged config (frac1.0, 60ep, patience12), same config all three = apples-to-apples curve.
close_reason: Not the objective. Subject-count sweep = characterization of a broken method, not a transfer fix; premature rigor on a near-chance result (bd memory early-stage-skip-rigor). Killed mid-run. Re-scoped Phase 1 to method iteration.

title: Strict balanced batching — equal trials per concept per batch (epic mindscape-dyq)
description: Replace the round-robin approximation (stratified_batches, which decays as scarce concepts drop out) with strict stratification: batch = concepts_per_batch x samples_per_concept. Each batch picks C concepts, draws exactly k trials from each (replacement for scarce concepts so counts stay equal). Guarantees uniform per-concept representation EVERY batch, whole epoch -> balanced InfoNCE negatives. Add concepts_per_batch/samples_per_concept to TrainConfig; new balanced_batches(concept_ids, C, k, rng) in sampling.py; unit-test the equal-count invariant.
close_reason: Code+tests on dev (sampling.py balanced_batches/stratified_batches/clip_hard_batches + TrainConfig knobs; test_sampling + test_nice). Parent epic dyq already closed same-basis. The GPU run to MEASURE the effect is the remaining work — that's the retrieval-quality problem itself, not this capability bead.

title: [epic] Balanced + hard contrastive training for EEG->image retrieval
description: Make the InfoNCE negatives BALANCED (equal trials per concept per batch) and HARD (CLIP-semantic + in-batch), which the v1 (epic 7tl) only approximated. The retrieval encoder currently trains on uniform-random batches: frequent concepts dominate, negatives are arbitrary. This epic is the proper implementation of the training-quality levers the owner specified. NO accuracy claim until a run (GPU-gated, deferred) — this is code+tests only. Supersedes the weak v1 pieces: ewd shipped round-robin (decays), pqh shipped uniform (unbalanced) subsampling; the strict versions live here.
acceptance_criteria: strict balanced batching (equal per-concept/batch), stratified subsampling, CLIP-prior hard-neg batches, and in-batch hard-neg weighting all implemented + unit-tested; a single optimized config ready for ONE good run when the GPU frees.
close_reason: All 4 children done (strict balanced batching, stratified subsample, CLIP-prior hard batches, in-batch hard-neg weighting) — code+tests on dev; the one good run is GPU-gated.

title: Cross-dataset montage/channel alignment — the blocker making D valid
description: cross_dataset_eval (EEG1->EEG2) returns CHANCE (0.57% vs 0.5%) while the EEG1 within-val is 2.17% (4x chance) — the encoder learns on EEG1 but transfers to nothing, because the runner assumes EEG1's 63 channels == EEG2's 63 in the SAME order/montage. They're different rigs; unaligned spatial filters land on wrong channels -> chance regardless of whether transfer is possible. CONFOUNDED, not a valid negative. Fix: map EEG1 electrode names to EEG2's montage order (or intersect the common channel set) so the encoder sees a consistent spatial layout across datasets; re-run. Until then D's number is uninformative and must NOT be reported as a finding.
acceptance_criteria: EEG1 and EEG2 channels aligned by electrode name/montage before cross-dataset eval; D re-run; result is interpretable (above-chance = transfer, chance = genuine null on aligned data).
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Interim checkpointing for multi-stage training runs — never lose completed work
description: A stall/crash mid-run (e.g. retrieval_audit's 4-encoder loop, cross_dataset_eval) loses ALL completed encoders because results are held in memory and only written to JSON at the very end. Today the qoa audit nearly lost 3 finished encoders when it stalled on the 4th. Fix: persist each stage's result (encoder state_dict + its metrics) to disk AS IT COMPLETES (runs/<name>/enc_<k>.pt + partial.json), and on startup RESUME from the last completed stage. Applies to retrieval_audit (per-encoder), cross_dataset_eval, and any harness loop that accumulates over folds/subjects. Optionally periodic in-epoch checkpoints for very long single trainings.
acceptance_criteria: retrieval_audit + cross_dataset_eval write per-stage checkpoints + resume from them; killing mid-run and restarting does not repeat completed stages.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Windowed stage-1 fNIRS decoder — stop collapsing the hemodynamic trajectory
description: Current fnirs_lda collapses the 22s/220-timepoint window to mean+slope+peak scalars/channel — provably lossy (our feature study: signal is the slope TRAJECTORY, not a global scalar). Build WindowedFnirs: slice window into sub-windows, per-window descriptors, stage-1 classifier per window, aggregate window-probs to block label. The cross-subject ~39% literature ceiling is measured ONLY on the collapsed representation; windowed is UNTESTED cross-subject on Shin. Evaluate within AND cross-subject vs collapse baseline, matched protocol. Composes with domain adaptation (mindscape-lpv) downstream.
close_reason: Built + swept. Keeping time helps within-subject (+2.5pp coarse concat, consistent) but is subject-idiosyncratic (ties collapse cross-subject; fine overfits). MIL pooling dead (smooth HRF, no localized cue). Reframes: cross-subject is distribution shift -> DA (mindscape-lpv) operates on this windowed rep; they compose.

title: Fix fold parallelism — harness runs folds serial (threading backend + GIL)
description: MEASURED (just the bottleneck cell, not full rerun): loky process backend FAILS here — n_jobs=-1 and n_jobs=8 both did 0 folds in 200-400s while workers visibly computed (MNE prints). NOT memory (56GB free). Cause: Windows spawns (not forks) loky workers -> each re-imports the heavy native stack (mne/scipy/mkl), then computes but never returns / respawns. Reverted to threading (only working fold-parallel backend here; GIL-caps GIL-bound FBCSP/CSP/ACM). KEY: sklearn cross_validate(n_jobs>1) uses the SAME loky backend -> mindscape-kvb is blocked by the same env limit. Fold-parallelism is a dead end on this Win+native setup. Working speedups: (1) less per-fold compute = downsample/crop (mindscape-241, changes results, needs re-record); (2) COARSE cell-level OS-process parallelism (independent reproduce_all --only chunks) -> grid floor = slowest single cell (FBCSP nback-cross 979s). No fine-grained fix. Closing as wontfix-in-env.
close_reason: RESOLVED with measurement (not wontfix). The 'loky workers never return / respawn = dead end' diagnosis was WRONG: guarded loky (parallel_config inner_max_num_threads=1) returns cleanly — the hang was oversubscription (N-proc×N-thread) or a bad entry context, not an env limit. Added a selectable fold backend to Harness.aggregate/run (backend='threading'|'loky'). But MEASURED, threading still wins at fold granularity on our classical zoo (FBCSP bnci 4-fold: threading 86.8s vs loky 121.4s): heavy inner ops are BLAS/C that scale with per-fold BLAS threads the guard must disable, + Windows spawn re-imports torch/mne per worker. So threading stays default (correct call, corrected reason); loky is a documented option for a future GIL-bound Python-level per-fold method. Coarse cell-level OS-process parallelism remains the real speedup.

title: Riemannian baseline (tangent space + MDM) parallel to CSP
description: pyRiemann tangent-space+LR and MDM in baselines/riemann.py, registered in neuroscan.models. Within-subject 0.706 (beats CSP 0.598 and the deep nets); cross-subject 0.357. neuroviz shows per-class Riemann discriminant channel weights under the decoder selector, coloring preserved. Tests: tests/unit/test_baselines_riemann.py. Ref ceilings added.
close_reason: Closed

title: Per-model augmentation policy (don't external-crop nets with internal aug)

Implement as the default standardizer for braindecode decoders. Likely the dominant missing preprocessing piece. See research/deep_dives/2026-06-30_2a_sota_recipe.md

title: Crop-augmentation decoding (the ATCNet 0.85 lever)

title: Perf: speed up perception trainings (GPU-resident data / compile / prefetch / early-stop delta)
description: Future perception trainings (train_nice.py) are ~28-42s/epoch on 240k trials, batch512 — the s/ep wobble is a CPU-contention tell (GPU starved on the per-batch numpy fancy-index gather + synchronous .to(device)). MEASURE-FIRST (r9b discipline): profile ONE epoch split into H2D copy / forward / backward / CPU gather; pick by what dominates, don't rewrite blind. Ranked priors: (T1 data path, biggest) GPU-resident fit-set — 240k x 63 x 200 x4B ~=12GB fits the 5090, load once + index_select per step -> zero H2D/gather; VRAM-GATE (9-subj LOSO ~38GB won't fit). Else pin_memory + non_blocking + double-buffer prefetch (generator paths have no workers today). (T2 compute, cheap) torch.compile(encoder); cudnn.benchmark (fixed shape) + TF32 — caveat cuDNN illegal-access at batch>=2048 on cu130 (line 48), test at 512; zero_grad(set_to_none=True). (T3 early-stop, saves epochs only when runs plateau) min_delta on the stop test (today strict val_top1>best_val, line 327, no delta -> noise resets patience); and stop on a CONTINUOUS signal (val InfoNCE loss / cos_to_true) not discrete val_top1 (1/N jumps). NOTE current runs are undertrained (val still climbing at cap) so early-stop is T3 not T1 — the wall-clock is in the data path. Parity-gate any change: same result, faster.
close_reason: TF32 lever landed + measured -22% step (backward-bound win); compile dead on Windows (no Triton), cudnn.benchmark neutral, data-path only 16% of step. GPU-resident deferred to follow-up (measured low headroom). See perf-tf32-training-lever memory.

title: MVNN per-subject calibration (leak-free) for NICE perception
description: The b40j MVNN win was a transductive leak (whitener fit on scored test trials w/ their condition labels). But the FIX I built (one global whitener pooled over train subjects, applied to test) cripples MVNN — a test subject's noise geometry != train-pool average (scored 1.44 < z-score 1.60). MVNN is meant per-subject (THINGS-EEG2/Guggenmos: each subject whitens by own noise cov). Do it RIGHT + leak-free: each subject's whitener fit on their OWN training-image trials (disjoint from the 200 scored test images), applied to that subject's trials. This is real-world unbatched deployment: enroll a subject once (calibration), then every incoming single trial = one fixed-matrix matmul. Measure clean per-subject MVNN vs z-score 1.60 for NICE; make default iff it wins. Supersedes the retraction framing.
close_reason: Per-subject leak-free MVNN built + measured: single-top1 1.66 vs z-score 1.60 (+0.06, within single-seed noise). The b40j +0.35 was ~85% leak. z-score stays default; MVNN kept as a correct, leak-free, documented override (mild ranking edge, concept-avg cost). No multi-seed per stage-gated rigor. interp: 2026-07-16_mvnn_per_subject_calibration.md

title: Prior-mining run-gate: forecast/number from mlruns + interpretations before spending GPU
description: FOUNDATION discipline (+ thin tooling). Before ANY training launch, mine the durable result corpus instead of running blind: (1) query mlruns for the closest prior runs (mlflow.set_tracking_uri('sqlite:///mlflow.db'); search_runs(search_all_experiments=True) -> params+metrics per run, e.g. metrics.test_single_top1, runName frozen_cbramod_topo_cnn_test5_s0); (2) read the matching interpretations/ doc for the sense-making + caveats; (3) predict the new result's direction + band from those priors + whether the code change plausibly moves it; (4) for a two-arm delta, NUMBER it from saved per-trial hits via paired bootstrap (neuroscan.tasks.visual.paired_delta / Metrics.boot_delta_ci) — no retrain. Only spend GPU when the forecast leaves a decision genuinely open AND no cheaper signal closes it. Deliverable: a small 'priors' query helper (closest mlruns runs + linked interpretation for an intent) + this gate documented as the pre-run checklist. Ties [[diagnose-run-worth-not-seeds]]. ACCEPTANCE: helper lists closest prior runs+metrics for an experiment intent; the multi-seed/harden beads are re-evaluated against it (below) and closed where priors+paired-bootstrap already answer them.
notes: FORECAST DELIVERED: interpretations/visual/2026-07-16_u9sv_next_lever_forecast.md. Post-geo queue prior-ranked: (1) extend ft/LoRA schedule (cheapest, proven capacity lever, both fts undertrained), (2) ooi ViT-L/14 target on ft encoder (richer-target x capacity; unblocks recon 71n), (3) 1x0->fNIRS n-back. DEFER: mnr (efficiency not floor-breaker), perception 1x0 step2/3 (structure priors wash on trainable EEG), further transfer-invariance (3 already null). Run-gate discipline: geo forecast to wash per 36g -> not multi-seeded on perception.
close_reason: Prior-mining run-gate delivered: next-lever forecast written + validated against the geo result (which it correctly predicted would wash). Queue recorded for the next GPU window.

title: Scale(/100) normalizer + fix CBraMod input chain, delete in-wrapper double-z (= pfad)
description: The pfad fix, done cleanly via the chain. Add core/normalization/scale.py (constant amplitude-preserving divide, e.g. /100 — confirm CBraMod's exact preprocessing from external/CBraMod: datasets do data/100, preprocessing/*.py mean-subtracts). CBraMod's default chain = [scale(1/100)] (+ any mean-subtract CBraMod used); DELETE the second z-score inside CBraModBackbone.forward (foundation.py) — the chain owns normalization now. Then measure: does amplitude-preserving input lift the frozen CBraMod probe off chance (0.6%) and/or the fine-tune/LoRA numbers, and does it reframe frozen-probe-loses? Depends on C1+C2 (the interface must exist).
acceptance_criteria: Scale normalizer + CBraMod amplitude-preserving default chain landed; foundation.py in-wrapper z-score removed; frozen-probe (+ short adapted) amplitude-vs-zscore measured; any reframing of frozen-probe-loses documented in interpretations/visual/; nox green
close_reason: Scale link + CBraMod in-wrapper z-score removed. Tested pfad hypothesis (pretraining microvolts/100 scale vs z-score) on frozen CBraMod probe: scale REGRESSED geometry heads (topo 1.75->1.21, pos_attn 1.59->1.23), flat unaffected. REFUTED at frozen probe -> CBraMod default reverted to z-score; Scale kept as --normalize override for the ft test (follow-up m3mi). Primary-source-confirmed pretraining=x/100 not z-score (deep-dive was wrong). Interpretation: 2026-07-15_cbramod_input_scale.md.

title: Normalizer interface + NormalizationChain + registry (core/normalization/normalization.py)
description: The strategy core. Define Normalizer (fit(X, ctx)->self ; apply(X)->X) where ctx carries optional groups(subject)/conditions(image)/fs/baseline-window; stateless links ignore ctx. NormalizationChain(Normalizer) = composite that fits+applies its links in order (fit threads ctx to each). A registry maps a config spec (list of names / {name:params} dicts) to a built chain, mirroring the encoder registry. No behaviour yet — just the interface, the composite, the registry, + test mirror at tests/unit/core/normalization/test_normalization.py (fit/apply contract, chain ordering, registry resolution).
acceptance_criteria: Normalizer interface + NormalizationChain composite + registry landed with test mirror; nox green
close_reason: core/normalization/ built: Normalizer strategy (apply(X,ctx)) + CompositeNormalization (list of normalizer objects, applied in order; NO registry per owner steer — objects constructed directly). Links ZScore/Mvnn/Scale. 13 tests, gates green.

title: Port z-score + MVNN onto the Normalizer interface; migrate adapter + train_nice to a chain spec
description: Move core/features/eeg/mvnn.py -> core/normalization/mvnn.py implementing Normalizer (fit the per-subject Sigma from ctx.groups/conditions, apply the whitener); add core/normalization/zscore.py (stateless per-channel). Replace the inline z-score + Normalize StrEnum in things_eeg2.py and the TrainConfig.mvnn bool + _load_split branching with a normalization CHAIN resolved from a config spec. NICE default chain = [mvnn] (preserve the current default). Move the mvnn test to the mirror path. Behaviour-preserving for NICE (single-trial must still reproduce ~1.94).
acceptance_criteria: zscore + mvnn are Normalizer impls under core/normalization/; adapter+train_nice consume a chain spec (mvnn bool + Normalize enum removed); NICE result unchanged; nox green
close_reason: Adapters (things_eeg2/eeg1) return RAW; normalization is the chain, resolved per-encoder by EncoderRegistry.normalization(model,override) constructing objects (NICE->MVNN, CBraMod/EEGPT->z-score). TrainConfig.mvnn bool + Normalize enum removed; _load_split/frozen_head/cross_dataset apply the chain; --normalize replaces --mvnn. nox green (323).

title: [epic] Normalization chain — composable Normalizer interface under core/normalization/ (absorbs pfad)
description:
Unify the scattered EEG preprocessing normalization behind ONE composable strategy interface. TODAY it is fragmented + branchy: per-channel z-score inline in the adapter (things_eeg2.py) gated by a Normalize StrEnum, MVNN in core/features/eeg/mvnn.py gated by a TrainConfig.mvnn bool, and CBraMod's expected /100 amplitude-scale + a SECOND in-wrapper z-score buried in foundation.py. That is exactly the low-ifs / strategy-pattern / one-home smell the house rules target.

DESIGN: a Normalizer interface with sklearn-shaped fit/apply — stateless per-trial links (z-score, scale /100) ignore the fit; data-fitted per-subject links (MVNN, recenter) fit a whitener from the data keyed by ctx.groups(subject)/ctx.conditions(image). A NormalizationChain is itself a Normalizer (composite) that fits+applies links in order. A registry (like the encoder registry) resolves a config spec (e.g. 'normalization': ['mvnn'] or [{'scale':0.01}]) to a chain — replacing TrainConfig.mvnn bool + the Normalize enum. Default chain becomes PER-ENCODER: NICE->[mvnn], CBraMod->[scale(1/100)] — which structurally fixes both the double-z bug (chain owns normalization, backbone stops re-normalizing) AND the MVNN-default-hurts-CBraMod caveat.

Lives under core/normalization/: normalization.py (interface + chain + registry), zscore.py, mvnn.py (moved), scale.py. Absorbs pfad (the CBraMod /100 fix = a Scale link + deleting foundation.py's in-wrapper z-score). Stretch member: fold Covariance.recenter_signals in as a Recenter normalizer (maximal unification, not a blocker). Sibling of the CLOSED Phase-0 epic 92jl under qoa — call it Phase 0.5 (foundation architecture).
close_reason: Normalization chain shipped: composable Normalizer interface + CompositeNormalization (objects, no registry), per-encoder chains, all consumers migrated, backbone in-wrapper z-scores removed. pfad absorbed + tested (refuted at frozen probe, ft follow-up m3mi). NICE->MVNN, CBraMod/EEGPT->z-score. nox green.

title: CBraMod input normalization: amplitude-preserving /100 vs our double z-score
description: FINDING from jwzl audit (2026-07-15): all 12 CBraMod finetune datasets (external/CBraMod/datasets/*.py) feed the model 'data/100' — CBraMod pretraining expected AMPLITUDE-PRESERVING input scaled by a fixed /100 divisor, NOT a z-score. Our perception path instead z-scores TWICE: per-channel per-epoch in the adapter (things_eeg2.py) then AGAIN inside CBraModBackbone.forward (foundation.py:88). Per-channel z-score destroys BOTH relative channel amplitude AND absolute scale — exactly the information CBraMod's pretrained (BatchNorm) stats encode. The adapter z-score was added as a bandaid for 'eval-mode embeddings collapse to chance', but BatchNorm collapses PRECISELY because it is fed off-distribution (wrong-scale) input its running stats never saw. HYPOTHESIS: feeding CBraMod amplitude-preserving /100-style input (matching pretraining) instead of z-score may (a) lift the frozen probe off chance (0.6%), (b) lift the fine-tune/LoRA numbers, and (c) REFRAME the 'frozen-probe-loses' conclusion (which may be partly an input-mismatch artifact, not purely 'clinical features do not transfer'). TEST: wire a 'cbramod'-style amplitude scaling (find CBraMod's exact per-sample preprocessing: preprocessing/*.py does mean-subtract; datasets do /100) as the normalize mode for the CBraMod path; run frozen probe + one short fine-tune arm, amplitude-preserving vs z-score, matched cross-subject. CAUTION (assume-wrong-if-too-good): verify the scale actually matches pretraining before claiming a lift; the z-score bandaid exists for a reason — confirm BatchNorm doesn't re-collapse.
acceptance_criteria: CBraMod amplitude-preserving input mode wired; frozen-probe + short fine-tune arm run amplitude-vs-zscore matched; keep/kill + any reframing of frozen-probe-loses documented in interpretations/visual/

title: Matched-rate NICE vs CBraMod — de-confound the ft-beats-NICE headline (resample 250 vs 200)
description: VALIDITY of our main claim. NICE headline (1.6% single-trial) ran at resample=250 (adapter/TrainConfig default); CBraMod ft/LoRA (2.38/2.10%) ran at 200 (CBraMod is hard-wired to 200Hz/1s patch). So 'fine-tune beats NICE' is at UNMATCHED input rate — a confound. Run NICE at resample=200 (matched to CBraMod), same cross-subject train[1-4]/test5 protocol, and re-compare. If NICE@200 stays ~1.6% the ft/LoRA win holds; if it moves, the headline needs restating. Cheap single run.
acceptance_criteria: NICE trained+evaluated at resample=200 on the matched cross-subject protocol; ft/LoRA-vs-NICE comparison restated at matched rate in the lora/foundation interpretation
close_reason: Premise (unmatched NICE@250 vs CBraMod@200) REFUTED by provenance: the ft-vs-NICE comparison was already matched at resample 200 (interp 2026-07-15_lora_cbramod.md:25 states resample 200 for all arms; foundation-frozen 2-subject comparison also 'matched trainer resample200'). The 250 was only the untouched TrainConfig DEFAULT, never the comparison rate. Fixed the real footgun instead: pinned resample:200 in all 12 perception configs so the CBraMod-required rate no longer rides on a CLI flag. No GPU run needed.

title: Preprocessing: match official THINGS-EEG2 (MVNN + pre-stim baseline) vs current z-score
description: DATA-AXIS, highest-value check. Current adapter (core/data/eeg/things_eeg2.py) uses tmin=0.0 (NO pre-stimulus baseline, NO baseline correction) + per-channel z-score over the 1s window; NO MVNN. The official THINGS-EEG2 pipeline (Gifford 2022) baseline-corrects + applies MVNN (multivariate noise normalization — whitens per-timepoint noise covariance), which is a documented reason NICE works. We hit the 14.8% anchor WITHOUT them, so neither is required — but MVNN+baseline is the obvious 'do we need more preprocessing' lever. WIRE MVNN + a pre-stim baseline window as opt-in adapter steps; run ONE matched cross-subject arm (train[1-4]/test5) MVNN+baseline vs current z-score. Keep-if-lifts, else document z-score is sufficient. Cheap single run, warm protocol.
acceptance_criteria: MVNN + baseline wired opt-in in the adapter; one matched cross-subject arm reported (MVNN+baseline vs z-score); keep/kill decision documented in interpretations/visual/
close_reason: MVNN wired (core/features/eeg/mvnn.py) + matched A/B run. MVNN beats z-score on cross-subject single-trial: top1 1.60->1.94 (+0.35pp), val +0.87, mean_rank -5, top5 +0.80, ~2x faster convergence; concept-avg top5 -2 (known whitening/averaging mechanism). KEEP opt-in, single-seed so multi-seed+LOSO follow-up filed (mindscape-x17a). Interpretation: interpretations/visual/2026-07-15_mvnn_preprocessing.md. Baseline-correction axis deliberately separate (needs pre-stim window). Foundation sound: z-score arm reproduces NICE 1.60 baseline.

title: Stage-3 foundation hardening — verify the base before new training elements
description: Audit (2026-07-15, Explore-verified) found the Stage-3 perception pipeline is largely SOUND: within-subject concept-avg = 14.8% reproduces published NICE 15.6% (same 200-way bank + averaging) => data->backbone->head->CLIP->eval validated end-to-end; the ~2% cross-subject single-trial is REAL task difficulty, not a pipeline bug. But four correctness/completeness smells vs the field-standard pipeline should be hardened before adding fancy training levers (good foundation > many fancy things). Children: MVNN+baseline preprocessing, matched-rate NICE-vs-CBraMod, double-z on the CBraMod path, LoRA prod-target test. Rule: these are correctness checks on an already-working base (stage-gated rigor: they harden a number that works), not finding-stage rigor.
close_reason: All 4 children resolved. Foundation verdict: base is SOUND (z-score arm reproduces NICE 1.60 baseline; within-subj concept-avg 14.8% reproduces published 15.6%). Findings: (b40j) MVNN beats z-score on single-trial +0.35pp+faster convergence, KEEP opt-in, multi-seed follow-up x17a; (9249) ft-vs-NICE already matched at 200, premise refuted, pinned configs; (jwzl) CBraMod wants amplitude-preserving /100 not z-score, follow-up pfad; (uz9v) LoRA prod-target test added. The ~2% cross-subject single-trial is real task difficulty, not a pipeline bug — cleared to build Phase 1 on it.

title: Adopt sdlc-scaffold v1.2.0: devtools becomes a pinned git-dep, drop vendored devtools/*.py
description:
sdlc-scaffold epic p99 (2026-07-15) extracts the devtools engines from vendored template source into an installable package, consumed via a git dependency pinned by tag: `devtools @ git+https://github.com/dimiturtrz/sdlc-scaffold.git@<tag>#subdirectory=sdlc-devtools`.

ACTION when scaffold v1.2.0 lands: run `copier update`. The update REMOVES the vendored devtools/*.py engines and ADDS the dep pin (devtools_ref var) + keeps the vendored ast-grep sgconfig/sg-rules + jscpd.json config data. Net: `python -m devtools.graph` (and the other engines) now run from the installed package; future engine updates are a one-line pin bump with NO devtools source diff in this repo's PRs (the churn this whole change kills).

This repo currently carries the v1.1.0 VENDORED copy of devtools/. P2 — do AFTER scaffold v1.2.0 is tagged.
notes:
--- RUNBOOK from mirage/synthscape-y3p (done 2026-07-15, commit 28194d5, PR #62) ---

1. git on PATH: copier shells out to git — `export PATH="/c/Program Files/Git/cmd:$PATH"` BEFORE the update, else it fails "Updating is only supported in git-tracked subprojects." (silent EXIT=1).
2. Command: `uvx copier update --defaults --trust --vcs-ref v1.2.0` from repo root, CLEAN tree first (commit the bd claim).
3. pyproject.toml WILL merge-conflict (UU). Template rewrites the `devtools` extra to the git-dep but collides with this repo's LOCAL [project.optional-dependencies] extras. Resolve: KEEP the new line `devtools = ["sdlc-devtools @ git+https://github.com/dimiturtrz/sdlc-scaffold.git@v1.2.0#subdirectory=sdlc-devtools"]` AND keep your project-specific extras; update any `all` aggregate to DROP grimp/networkx and ADD the sdlc-devtools pin. Verify no <<<<<<< markers remain (use the Grep tool, NOT recursive shell grep — it hangs on .venv).
4. Expected deletions (NOT data loss): devtools/*.py, devtools/sgconfig.yml, sg-rules/, jscpd.json, tests/unit/devtools/*. Config now ships IN the package — locate via `uv run python -m devtools.config sgconfig|jscpd` (prints the site-packages path).
5. 0 code translations expected — but verify: no repo code imports `devtools` (grep `from devtools`/`import devtools` under your pkg dirs; noxfile refs are fine). mirage had 0.
6. Docs to fix (PROJECT-OWNED, keep CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md in sync): CLAUDE.md static-analysis gate table (devtools/graph.py -> `python -m devtools.graph`; sgconfig/jscpd -> package config via `python -m devtools.config`; shape_contracts path) + the "Scaffolding" note (devtools/ no longer vendored, now the sdlc-devtools pinned git-dep). AGENTS.md gate commands: `ast-grep ... -c "$(uv run python -m devtools.config sgconfig)"` and jscpd `--config "$(uv run python -m devtools.config jscpd)"`. LEAVE template-owned ci.yml + pyproject comments to the scaffold (they still name devtools/graph.py — the template's call, not yours).
7. Verify: `uv sync --all-extras` FIRST (bare `uv run pytest` prunes extras -> open3d/torch missing, false failures). Then `uvx nox -s lint` (clean), `uv run --no-sync pytest tests -q` (test count DROPS by the # of removed devtools tests — mirage: 203 -> 150, ~53 gone; expect similar), and `uv run --no-sync python -m devtools.graph --assert <pkgs>` + `... devtools.shape_contracts --assert <pkgs>` clean FROM THE PACKAGE.
8. PR: `gh pr edit` may fail on token org-scope (needs read:org) -> fallback `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<n> -X PATCH -F body=@bodyfile.md`.
9. Grunt work (import-ref scout + gate/test sweep) parallelizes cleanly onto Haiku subagents — phrase as run+report-raw, not analysis.
close_reason: Adopted sdlc-scaffold v1.2.0 (commit 126ef12). devtools engines now the installed sdlc-devtools==1.2.0 git-dep (pinned by devtools_ref @v1.2.0, subdirectory=sdlc-devtools) — vendored devtools/*.py + sgconfig/sg-rules/jscpd.json + tests/unit/devtools/* all removed; configs ship in package (python -m devtools.config). pyproject conflict resolved (git-dep + all local extras kept). CLAUDE.md+AGENTS.md gate table updated in sync. 0 code import translations. Verified: uv sync --all-extras, nox -s lint green from package, pytest 309 (was 357, 48 devtools tests moved to scaffold), graph/shape --assert clean. Future engine updates = one-line devtools_ref bump, no source diff. PR #55.

title: Rework braindecode model selection: getattr(bd_models, arch.cls) string-zoo lookup -> validated registry
description: neuroscan/models/decoders.py resolves a braindecode model class by NAME string from arch config: net_cls = getattr(bd_models, arch.cls). Fragile — no validation (a typo'd arch.cls is an AttributeError at train time, not config-load), IDE/refactor can't follow the string, and any braindecode symbol is reachable (not just intended model classes). Replace with an explicit validated registry: a {name: class} map (or pydantic-validated Literal of allowed arch names) so the allowed set is closed, checked at config time, and greppable. Surfaced in PR #52 review ('net cls? getattr?! open beads task to unfuck this setup').
acceptance_criteria: arch.cls resolved via an explicit closed registry/enum with config-time validation; no getattr on a module by user-supplied string
close_reason: Replaced getattr(bd_models, arch.cls) string-zoo with explicit _BD_CLASSES registry + BraindecodeClf.resolve() (validated, KeyError lists registered set). Unified profile.py onto the same resolver (dropped its parallel getattr + unused import). Tests: all MODELS cls -> nn.Module subclass + unknown-class KeyError. nox -s lint green. Commit eeabbbd.

title: Adopt sdlc-scaffold (>= v0.10.0) — register + retrofit slots + slot-scope the gates

title: Backfill continuous metrics across existing arms + error-distribution readout
description: Once evaluate() emits the continuous metrics, rerun the frozen head zoo off the disk feature-cache (mean/flat/pos_attn/topo/gcn, ~1min/arm) + NICE + CBraMod-ft to populate cos-to-true/margin/mean-rank. Produce a readout: for each arm, is the near-miss distribution 30deg-off or 120deg-off, and does a higher-accuracy arm also have tighter angular error. This is the diagnostic the accuracy table can't give.
close_reason: Backfilled continuous metrics across the frozen CBraMod head zoo (mean/flat/pos_attn/topo_g24/gcn, 120ep off disk cache) + readout in learning/2026-07-14_perception_continuous_metrics.md. Findings: every arm ~orthogonal to true concept (cos 0.001-0.058 = arccos ~86-90deg, incl best topo 1.75%); raw cos/z geometry-confounded (all z -3.0..-3.6, EEG embeddings sit outside the tight CLIP concept cluster - random-pair cos 0.334); MARGIN + MEAN_RANK track accuracy (mean_rank 97->70, margin 0.001->0.017), raw cos does not; retrieval lives in a tiny positive margin. Higher accuracy != tighter angular error (cos_std ~0.02-0.03 flat). NICE/ft ride their next train (no dedicated retrain). Epic 2y7k complete: metric (.1) + interpretable reference (.2) + backfill/readout (.3).

title: Concept-space angle reference — random-cos baseline so absolute cos-to-true is interpretable
description: CLIP concept vectors aren't evenly spread (animals cluster), so an absolute cos-to-true of e.g. 0.4 needs a reference. Compute the distribution of pairwise cos over the 200 test-concept vectors (mean/std/percentiles) = 'what's a random cos in this space'. Report cos-to-true as both raw AND relative-to-random (z or percentile). Fixed per split, computed once from clip_targets.
close_reason: Concept-space random-cos reference built INTO retrieval_continuous (DRYer than a separate clip_targets method — the candidate bank IS the concept prototypes, so the reference is its off-diagonal cosines; works for val + test banks, no CLIP-reload). Added random_cos_mean/std + cos_to_true_z (sigma above the random-concept-pair baseline). Real number on the 200 test concepts: random_cos 0.334+-0.092 (NOT ~0 — CLIP concepts strongly cluster, pairs up to 0.80), so absolute cos-to-true is not self-interpretable; z fixes it. Demonstrated value: NICE 2ep smoke raw cos 0.079 looks positive but z=-2.8 (BELOW random floor, undertrained near-noise embeddings). Unit-tested (perfect z>5, chance |z|<3, clustered-space raises the bar).

title: topo_cnn grid/sigma mini-sweep — close the head axis honestly
description:
topo_cnn (RBF scalp-grid + 2D CNN) is the frozen ceiling (~1.68) but its two knobs — _GRID (scalp image size, 16) and _RBF_SIGMA (interp width, 0.2) — were never tuned (picked once). Small sweep (e.g. GRID in {12,16,24} x SIGMA in {0.1,0.2,0.35}) on the CACHED CBraMod features to confirm 1.68 isn't left on the table by the interpolation resolution. Cheap because it rides the disk cache (task 1) — head-only fits, no backbone forward.

WALL-TIME: ~3-6min GPU total (up to 9 head fits x ~60ep on cached feats, seconds/epoch). Expected effect <0.1 (band is sticky — this CLOSES the axis, doesn't reopen it). If a knob moves it >=0.1, that's a real surprise worth a second seed. Depends on task 1 (the cache/param substrate).
notes: Topo grid×sigma sweep DONE (test5, 60ep, off cache, 27m). grid|s01|s02|s035: g12 1.42/1.62/1.61, g16 1.64/1.66/1.68, g24 1.68/1.79/1.57. Most <=1.68 ceiling (confirms head axis ~closed). ONE spike: g24_s02=1.79 (+0.11 over 1.68) clears the >=0.1 bar -> licenses a multi-seed confirm (seed0 gave it; running seeds 1,2). Lone spike (neighbors 1.68/1.57), val 3.43% doesn't corroborate -> likely single-seed scatter, but the rule says confirm. If it holds across seeds -> a real head lever; if it regresses to ~1.68 -> scatter, axis stays closed.
close_reason: Topo grid/sigma sweep + multi-seed confirm DONE. Sweep (test5,60ep): grid|s01/s02/s035 = g12 1.42/1.62/1.61, g16 1.64/1.66/1.68, g24 1.68/1.79/1.57. g24_s02=1.79 cleared the >=0.1 bar -> multi-seed confirm: seeds 0/1/2 = 1.79/1.75/1.81 = 1.78+-0.03, all 3 above the 1.68 ceiling = REAL, not scatter. Finer scalp-grid (24 vs 16px) resolves topography grid16 blurred. Frozen-CBraMod ceiling 1.68->~1.78 (+0.10). First head tweak to move single-trial since topo_cnn. Still < ft 2.3. Head axis NOT fully closed - grid resolution was a real lever. Recommend: make g24/s02 the topo default; the m69x.3 backbone swap now compares against ~1.78.

title: Apply bootstrap delta-CI to the ft-vs-NICE single-trial gap (830 without folds)
description: Epic 82ow. Once boot_delta_ci exists, answer the deferred 830 question WITHOUT LOSO folds: does CBraMod-ft (~2.28% single-trial top1, test5) beat NICE (~1.6%) beyond noise? Re-score the existing ft + NICE single-split runs on the SAME test trials (reuse cached embeddings — no retrain), take the paired bootstrap delta CI on single-trial top1. If the 95% CI excludes 0, the lift is real; if it straddles 0, the ~0.7pp gap is scatter. This is 'harden the win' honestly from one run each — honors the owner's anti-fold / stage-gated-rigor rule. Report point [lo,hi] for ft, NICE, and the paired delta. Depends on the boot_ci task.
close_reason: Tool shipped (paired_delta CLI + boot_ci/boot_delta_ci, tested+gated). Empirical run deliberately NOT fired: power arithmetic makes the verdict foregone and the run can't reach the real uncertainty. n=16224 test trials, gap ~0.7pp (ft 2.3 vs NICE 1.6), per-arm SE ~0.11pp -> paired delta ~5sigma, p<1e-6 -> test-noise CI excludes 0 near-certainly. But a test-TRIAL bootstrap captures test-set sampling noise ONLY, not training-SEED variance -> 'real' would mean 'beyond test noise for these two encoders', not seed-robust. The axis that could actually be scatter (seed) is out of scope (anti-fold/multi-seed veto). No cached checkpoints existed so it needed 2 fresh trains (~40min GPU) to confirm a ~5sigma foregone number on an axis that doesn't answer the open question. Stage-gated-rigor applied to self: banked the wall-time. Tool + estimate is the deliverable.

CONCRETE: (1) expose per-trial hits — Nice.retrieval_topk (nice.py:108) already computes the boolean hit vector (order[:,:k]==labels).any(dim=-1) then discards it with .mean(); return the vector (a retrieval_hits() sibling or an optional return) so it can be resampled. (2) Add boot_ci(hits, n_boot, alpha) -> (point, lo, hi) percentile bootstrap, and boot_delta_ci(hits_a, hits_b) paired over the SAME test trials -> gap CI (resample shared indices, cancels item noise). (3) Wire into the retrieval/harness metrics output ('point [lo, hi]' like synthscape). Unit tests (equivalence-class: known hit rate -> CI brackets it; paired delta on identical arms -> CI straddles 0). NO folds, NO retrains.

title: test-mirror: adopt cardioseg strict path-mirror (.exists at tests/unit/<path>/test_<name>.py)
description:
Mindscape's test-mirror sub-rule (devtools/graph.py unmirrored, bd 9nj) matches LOOSE: a source module counts as mirrored if imported by SOME test anywhere. Owner reviewed a PR and found this weird/wrong — wants the cardioseg WAY: a strict path-mirror where tests/unit/<same path>/test_<name>.py must EXIST on disk. Cardioseg's unmirrored() is pure Path(test_root)/f.parent/f'test_{f.name}'.exists(), test_root=tests/unit, only __init__/__main__ exempt, backfilled to 0 (bd j4m2), BLOCKING.

WHY loose is wrong: it accepts a logic module tested only indirectly (via a baselines/integration test) with no dedicated unit test at its mirror path — exactly what looked weird in the PR. Strict forces one home per module: tests/unit mirrors the source tree method-wise.

SCOPE (measured 2026-07-13): 44 modules lack strict mirror. 9 are real LOGIC (need real mirror tests, no stubs): core/data/eeg/base.py, core/data/fnirs/base.py, core/data/signal.py, core/features/fnirs/chromophore.py, core/features/fusion/coupling.py, neuroscan/evaluation/harness.py, neuroscan/evaluation/results.py, neuroscan/tasks/workload/_eval.py, neuroscan/tasks/workload/feature_importance/_cv.py. 35 are coverage-omit shells (GPU/download/runner/adapter glue).

DEVIATION from pure cardioseg (deliberate, flagged): KEEP the coverage-omit shell exemption. Cardioseg has ~3 omit shells; mindscape has 35 genuinely-non-unit-testable shells. Pure cardioseg (only __init__/__main__ exempt) forces 35 stub tests = violates the no-stubs house rule. So: strict .exists() MECHANISM (cardioseg) + omit exemption for declared shells (mindscape's principled carve). unmirrored() keeps reading [tool.coverage] omit.

DONE = unmirrored() rewritten to mirror.exists() at tests/unit/<path>/test_<name>.py; 9 real mirror tests backfilled at exact paths (real assertions, no stubs); test_graph.py updated (strict semantics); gate proven exit 1 on a missing mirror then 0 once backfilled; CI green; CLAUDE.md+AGENTS.md synced; the 7 existing 9nj tests relocated to mirror paths if not already there.

title: Guardrail superset: match cardiac-seg / synthscape gate state
description:
Adopt the 3 static-analysis gates mindscape lacks but both sibling repos (cardiac-seg, synthscape/surfscan) enforce, + harmonize existing gates to the shared superset. Target state = synthscape's (a fellow CONSUMER of the pattern, not a scaffold source).

CURRENT (all enforced): ruff-ratchet(y63), vulture two-tier, import-linter(o92), coverage>=80.
MISSING (the goal): arch-fitness (graph.py --assert, bd 3nn), ast-grep (bd ylq), jscpd (bd apl). Harmonize existing to superset (bd jmy). Scaffold machinery (nox kvo, pre-commit dno) = stretch; copier (wxs) = deferred separate project.

=== NO SHORTCUTS (binding rule for every child task) ===
Each gate must be REAL and reached the hard way — no dodges:
1. FIX-ALL-FIRST, then enforce. Reach zero violations by FIXING the code, never by excluding paths, loosening thresholds, or adding blanket ignores to get green. The ratchet only ever TIGHTENS.
2. NO advisory-forever cop-out. Advisory is a temporary staging step (prove the CI wiring), with a written trigger to graduate to blocking. A gate parked advisory to avoid the fix does NOT count as done.
3. NO suppression-to-pass. Suppressions (noqa/ignore/omit) stay minimal + meaningful — none that suppress nothing (RUF100-style), none that hide a real hit instead of fixing it. Every suppression carries a one-line why.
4. DON'T close a gate task on 'config added' or 'builds'. Close only after the gate RUNS in CI and is observed to BLOCK a real (planted or found) violation, then pass once fixed. Build+test != done.
5. NO threshold-lowering to pass. file_max/bottleneck_degree/coverage-floor move only UP (tighter), never relaxed to accommodate bad code.
6. Report faithfully: if a gate can't reach clean, say so with the violation list — don't quietly weaken it.
close_reason: All children closed (3nn arch-fitness, ylq ast-grep, apl jscpd, jmy harmonize, kvo nox, dno pre-commit). Superset now enforced in CI via sdlc-scaffold union-select adoption (landed wholesale v0.14-v1.10). Goal state = synthscape parity reached.

title: Reconstruction objective: InfoNCE + MSE-to-CLIP, larger CLIP target (ViT-L/14)
description: Endpoint is image RECONSTRUCTION, not just retrieval. Current target = open_clip ViT-B/32 512-d L2-normed, loss = InfoNCE (contrastive, direction-only). Retrieval top-k measures 'right nearest neighbor' — necessary but not sufficient for faithful decoder conditioning. Add an MSE-to-CLIP-target term (hit the actual embedding, not just its direction) so the predicted vector is a usable conditioning latent. Also ViT-B/32 (512-d) is coarse for sharp recon; MindEye-class work uses ViT-L/14 (768-d)+ — swap the target for recon fidelity. Measure both retrieval AND embedding-MSE.
notes: RECON ARM RAN (cbramod_ft + OpenAI ViT-L/14 + mse_weight1.0, train1-4/test5, 75ep, best-val ep61): single-top1 1.82 / top5 7.60, concept-top1 2.50 / top5 13.50, emb_mse 0.00253. VERDICT: recon objective COSTS retrieval vs vitb32-ft baseline (2.38 / 9.58 / 4.5 / 20.0) — as u9sv forecast anticipated (richer target + MSE orthogonal-to / costs top-k). emb_mse 0.00253 looks tiny but on 768-d L2 unit vectors = cos~0.03 = NEAR-ORTHOGONAL (the established faint-tilt hard floor, perception-continuous-metrics), NOT real embedding proximity -> mse term did not overcome the SNR floor. Confounds target-swap + mse; a vitl14-no-mse control would separate them (follow-up). The encoder is decodable (768 OpenAI-CLIP space) — feeds 71n. Expect decode(EEG) weak / decode(true CLIP) = decoder ceiling = the honest cross-subject recon gap.
close_reason: Recon objective (ViT-L/14 target zoo + MSE-to-CLIP + emb_mse) built + evaluated. Verdict: COSTS retrieval vs vitb32-ft (single-top1 1.82 vs 2.38); emb_mse 0.00253 = cos~0.03 near-orthogonal (the SNR hard floor, not real embedding proximity). Not a retrieval lever, as u9sv forecast. KEPT as opt-in infra — its real value delivered 71n (decodable OpenAI-ViT-L/14 head). z-score/vitb32 stay retrieval defaults.

title: LoRA / low-rank CBraMod adaptation — cheap sub-patch temporal unlock
description: CBraMod is 200Hz/1s-patch: for our 1s stimulus the whole trial is ONE patch (S=1), so fine ERP timing is COMPRESSED inside the frozen patch embedding and unreachable by any head or by resampling (breaks the baked-in rfft spectral embedding). The ONLY way to expose sub-patch temporal signal is to ADAPT the backbone weights. Full fine-tune works (2.38%) but is the expensive/low-yield lever. LoRA = cheap low-rank adaptation of the attention/proj matrices — adapts the patch representation to preserve task-temporal info at a fraction of the params/compute. Test vs frozen-head ceiling + full-ft 2.38%.
close_reason: KEEP. Rank-8 LoRA on CBraMod FFN (linear1/linear2, 10.2% trainable, attention frozen/unreachable) reaches test5 single-trial top1 2.10% (matched train[1-4]/test5) vs NICE 1.60 / full-ft 2.38 / frozen 0.63(chance) — beats NICE on all 4 metrics, matches ft concept-top1, beats ft concept-top5, ~64% of ft-over-NICE gap. Sub-patch temporal reachable by cheap low-rank FFN adaptation, not only full ft. PEFT rejected (dep for same FFN-only nn.Linear subset, can't reach fused MHA). Mechanism: neuroscan/models/lora.py + unified Model.param_groups. Interpretation: interpretations/visual/2026-07-15_lora_cbramod.md. 2.10 vs 2.38 within single-seed scatter -> cheap ALTERNATIVE confirmed; hardening (multiseed/LOSO/rank-sweep) = follow-up.

title: Geometry-aware frozen head: topo-CNN / pos-attn / GCN over electrode positions
description: Frozen CBraMod head-search (nm5) found: mean/attn pool = chance, flat (unordered bag of 63 channel-tokens) = ~1.2% single/3.0% concept. Spatial PRESERVATION is the lever, not head depth. But flat ignores electrode GEOMETRY (permutation-arbitrary). Fold the 63 tokens by their scalp 2D positions: (1) pos_attn = electrode-position embedding + self-attention, (2) topo_cnn = RBF-interp features onto a scalp grid + 2D CNN (Bashivan 2016), (3) GCN = electrode-adjacency message passing. All frozen-backbone, cheap. Beat flat (1.2%) and NICE (1.6%) frozen = spatial structure was the missing signal + a cheap deploy (no backbone training). Built in frozen_head.py; sweep running.
design: Matched frozen sweep, train[1,2,3,4]->test5, S=1/200Hz (correct rate), 120ep. Arms: mean_lin(floor) < flat_mlp(bag ref) vs pos_attn, topo_cnn. Positions from eeg_positions(channels()) unit-disk montage.
notes: First geometry sweep done (PR #44): topo_cnn 1.68% single / 4.50% concept BEST, beats NICE frozen, all arms undertrained (val climbing ep118/120). Remaining: longer schedule (heads not converged), GCN arm, tune grid(16)/sigma(0.2), then full LOSO + multi-seed (830).
close_reason: Geometry frozen-head sweep + GCN arm + longer schedule (300ep, test5). Results single-trial top1: topo_cnn 1.57 / pos_attn 1.45 / gcn 1.07 / flat 1.15 / mean_lin 0.64. TWO NEGATIVES: (1) longer schedule does NOT help — topo_cnn 1.57 (300ep) <= 1.68 (120ep, PR#44); heads converge early (topo best-ep171) then overfit, the 'undertrained val-climbing' read was wrong. (2) GCN (electrode-adjacency message passing) is the WEAKEST geometry head (1.07 << topo 1.57) — adjacency mixing loses to RBF-grid CNN + position-attention. topo_cnn stays the best frozen head ~1.6 = at NICE, below ft ~2.3. Combined w/ o2n: single-trial top1 unmoved by schedule OR geometry-head OR GCN — sits in a ~1.6-2.3 band, real wall. GCN arm + ft_long config committed (221172d). 830 folds stay deferred (nothing moved the number to harden).

title: Fast head-lever fine-tune: attention pool vs mean-pool bottleneck (CBraMod perception)
description: The winning fine-tuned CBraMod (n3q, single 2.38%) collapses the backbone's [B,C,S,d] token grid with a FLAT MEAN over (C,S) before the CLIP head — maximally lossy, yet still beats NICE. Fast head-side levers (warm-startable, ~1hr/60ep on train4, compare to existing 2.38%): (1) attention pool over C*S tokens (cbramod_ft_attn, undo the bottleneck); (2) crude single-LR longer schedule (n3q was undertrained, best_val at ep59/60). Also fixed a confound: default backbone_lr_scale was 0.1 (07m discriminative-LR = the LOSING 2.10% arm); restored to 1.0 (n3q winning single-LR).
design: Matched arms train[1,2,3,4]->test5, lr1e-4, perception_converged recipe. Baseline = n3q crude single 2.38%/concept 4.5%. Arm A: cbramod_ft_attn 60ep. Arm B: cbramod_ft 120ep (undertraining test). Keep-or-kill on single-trial top1 vs 2.38%.
close_reason: Frozen head-search delivered: geometry heads (topo_cnn 1.68% > pos_attn 1.59% > flat 1.21% > mean 0.60%) beat from-scratch NICE frozen; spatial-preservation + electrode-geometry is the lever. PR #44. Geometry productionization continues in j4l.

title: Stage-3 perception: pretrained EEG foundation-model encoder — capacity vs SNR floor
description:
Decisive test of the ONE untested perception axis (encoder capacity/priors). Replace the 693k from-scratch NICE conv encoder with a PRETRAINED EEG foundation transformer (LaBraM/EEGPT-class) behind the same [B,C,T]->L2-normed[B,D] CLIP-space contract, run through the existing train_nice trainer + retrieval eval. NICE stays as the baseline arm; the foundation model is the treatment arm — matched train-4/test-5 cross-subject single-trial retrieval.

WHY: three from-scratch transfer levers (dpi input-align, 36g adversarial, lbd soft-InfoNCE) all null (memory perception-cross-subject-hard-floor). The remaining axis is capacity/priors — but scaling NICE from scratch overfits at n<=17 subj (nice.py says so). Foundation pretraining moves capacity OFF the tiny labeled set and supplies subject-general priors the invariance losses couldn't learn from 4 subjects. This is the field's actual big-model recipe.

DECISIVE EITHER WAY: foundation beats NICE cross-subject single-trial -> capacity/priors was the limit (real win). It doesn't -> strong evidence the floor is single-trial SNR physics, not the encoder (clean publishable negative). Backbone code lives checked-out under external/ (pinned, not vendored). Placement: neuroscan/models/, swappable via a TrainConfig model field — NOT baselines/.
close_reason: DELIVERED — foundation-model encoder test complete. Verdict: fine-tuned CBraMod (pretrained EEG transformer) BEATS from-scratch NICE on cross-subject perception, replicated across 2 held-out subjects (single-trial top1 2.38/2.04% vs 1.60/1.66%, ~1.35x; all 8 metric comparisons favor ft). Frozen probe LOSES (0.63%, domain shift) -> only adapted capacity cashes it. CONCLUSION: the wall is NOT a pure single-trial SNR floor — a bigger pretrained encoder lifts it; first method in the arc to beat NICE. Both ft runs under-trained (lower bound). Built: encoder registry seam (h1p), external CBraMod checkout MIT (yra), foundation wrapper (yjd), matched eval (n3q). Hardening (full LOSO+multi-seed, longer schedule, discriminative-lr/LoRA) filed as follow-ups 830/o2n/07m. Memory: foundation-frozen-probe-loses, foundation-checkpoint-choice.

title: Concept-aware InfoNCE — kill same-concept false negatives
description: Same-concept-different-image pairs in a batch are treated as negatives though their CLIP targets are ~0.7 similar. Soft targets or a concept-aware sampler removes this contradictory gradient. Cheap single-run test.
close_reason: Concept-aware soft-target InfoNCE (soft_tau, CLIP-sim soft labels) implemented + tested. Decode: single1 1.73 (single-seed, below 1.87 baseline mean = washes). Third lever to fail. Confirms the cross-subject single-trial floor is SNR/capacity-bound, robust to input-align/adversarial/loss-quality. Kept opt-in. See bd memory perception-cross-subject-hard-floor.

title: Domain-adversarial subject-invariance (gradient reversal on subject id)

title: [epic] Retrieval encoder — training efficiency, representation + metrics
description: Unify the EEG->image retrieval-training improvements surfaced during the qoa audit: the 0.69M NICE encoder is fed 240k trials/epoch (over-sized, bandwidth-bound ~43s/epoch fp32), batches are uniform-random (unbalanced InfoNCE negatives), and we report only top-1/5 (discarding rank). Four coordinated levers: (1) per-epoch trial subsampling for speed, (2) stratified concept-balanced batching, (3) smart/hard-negative selection, (4) richer retrieval metrics. (Cross-dataset montage alignment (mindscape-fji) is tracked separately — it's a correctness blocker for D, not a training improvement.)
design: GOAL: fast + concept-balanced + rank-instrumented cross-subject retrieval trainer. 3 deliverables, NONE bet on top-k gain: (1) bf16+subsample >=2x faster epoch at MATCHED final top1 (parity is the guard). (2) MRR/mAP/PR-AUC on every report - permanent honest-reporting value. (3) negatives (ewd balanced-batch + mnr hard-neg) ABLATED vs random-batch to a keep/kill verdict, NOT shipped on faith - prior lbd washed 1.73v1.87, forecast wash vs SNR floor; >noise(+-0.14) = surprise worth multiseed. NON-GOAL: beat 1.6-2.4 floor (wall=capacity/SNR, only adaptation moved it per o2n). Done: faster@parity + rank-aware numbers + documented ablation writeup.
acceptance_criteria: the four member tasks land with ablations; the retrieval trainer is faster (bf16 + subsample), batches are concept-balanced, hard negatives improve cross-subject retrieval, and every retrieval number carries MRR/mAP/PR-AUC.
close_reason: DELIVERED (2026-07-18) as an efficiency+instrumentation epic, no top-k gain (correctly forecast). (1) SPEED: bf16 3.1x + subsample frac0.2 =~15x fp32 at IDENTICAL top1 1.82 - free win, parity holds. (2) METRICS: MRR/median-rank/PR-AUC wired into primary evaluate() (was cross_dataset-only), committed. (3) NEGATIVE-QUALITY: balanced(ewd)+clip_hard(mnr) ABLATED = null (1.77/1.71 vs 1.82, within noise, slower) -> killed as defaults, kept opt-in. Wall is capacity/SNR not negative-selection (4th confirming axis). Original acceptance clause 'hard negatives improve retrieval' FALSIFIED - they wash. interp 2026-07-18_retrieval_trainer_speed_and_negative_ablation.md

Should improve the learned representation (esp. the many-exemplar THINGS concepts) + makes the InfoNCE negatives principled. Ablate vs uniform.

title: [epic] Adopt the code-quality gate — ruff ratchet + conventions (port from cardiac-seg)
description:
Port the CI code-quality gate + conventions proven in cardiac-seg (PRs #1-4). mindscape is GREENFIELD (ci.yml has a tests job, NO lint step, no [tool.ruff]). Lint paths: core neuroscan (neuroviz is TS/excluded).

INTEGRATION PATH (the cardiac-seg-proven ratchet):
1. CI: add a lint job to .github/workflows/ci.yml, required alongside tests on dev->main (branch protection, strict). Pin ruff 0.15.13 via uvx. TWO steps: ENFORCED (blocks; the families already at 0, via --select subset) + ADVISORY full (ruff check . --statistics with a trailing || true, non-blocking, surfaces the rest).
2. pyproject [tool.ruff]: line-length 120, extend-exclude the TS/data/staging dirs; [tool.ruff.lint].select = the curated set (decided rule-by-rule, NOT select ALL).
3. RATCHET each family to 0 by FIXING (owner: fix-all philosophy), then graduate it into the enforced select. ORDER (cardiac-seg proven): F,I (imports/unused/undefined = real bugs) -> B (bugbear; B009 catches getattr-with-constant) -> T201 (no prints, use logging) -> PLR2004 (name magic numbers) -> PLC0415 (imports at top; fix circular imports by EXTRACTION, never lazy imports) -> BLE001 + S110 (no blind except: specific exception or let it crash; know what CAN throw) -> C901 + PLR0912/0915 (complexity: split / strategy pattern) -> PLR0913 (too-many-args: config objects) -> FBT (bools keyword-only) -> RUF100 (no dead noqa). E501 stays advisory if a dense hand-style makes 120 painful.

CONVENTIONS (owner guidance from the cardiac-seg review threads):
- noqa: bare form only, NO prose reasons. RUF100 enforces that a noqa suppresses a REAL violation.
- Owner is AGAINST COMMENTS: minimal, self-documenting code + good names; no explanatory prose (a comment jammed mid-expression once silently killed code = a real bug).
- getattr/hasattr = smell: prefer direct attribute access; B009 catches the no-default form; the with-default form + hasattr need review (no ruff rule).
- CONFIG POLYMORPHISM (IF the project has strategy-selecting configs): make them pydantic DISCRIMINATED UNIONS where each variant owns its params + a virtual build() that instantiates its object (cfg.build()), not a kind/type dispatch. kind = serialization discriminator only. Add a --set variant shim + a _lift backward-compat validator for old flat configs.

title: Unify EEG adapter interface — heterogeneous labels as nullable meta columns
description: The DatasetAdapter Protocol (core/data/eeg/base.py) is classification-shaped: name/n_classes/label_map + get_data->(X, y, meta) with y a canonical class int. It doesn't fit RETRIEVAL (THINGS-EEG2: the per-epoch target is a CLIP embedding via concept+image, not a class) — so things_eeg2.py is currently a plain module with its own get_epochs, outside the common interface. As we add heterogeneously-labelled EEG datasets (MI/workload=class, image=retrieval, text=seq2seq), we need one interface spanning them. Raised in PR #21 review ('isnt there a common interface here?').
design:
Core reframe: the SIGNAL side is common (epochs + subject/session/run + stimulus keys); the LABEL is what's heterogeneous. So don't enrich a positional y — fold the label into meta as NAMED NULLABLE columns (the cardioseg 'unknown -> null' pattern). Adapter returns (X, meta); tasks read the columns they need.

meta schema: core {subject,session,run} always present; label columns nullable per paradigm: class_label:int|null (MI 0-3, workload 0-2), stimulus_id:str|null (image file/word), concept:int|null (semantic idx), target_kind:str ('class'|'retrieval'|...). Classification: y=meta['class_label']; retrieval: meta['stimulus_id']->CLIP + meta['concept'] for eval; new paradigm = add a nullable column, no interface change.

OPTIONS considered:
A. Labels-as-nullable-meta (cardioseg) — RECOMMENDED. Drop positional y, adapters return (X, meta), labels in columns. Cost: refactor base.py + bnci/shin adapters + the eval harness (reads meta['class_label']).
B. Typed target payload + kind tag: (X, target, meta), target dataset-typed. Smaller but consumers dispatch on kind.
C. Decouple epochs from labels: adapter gives (X, keys, meta), task-side labeler maps keys->target. Subset of A (A's meta IS the keys); THINGS-EEG2 already fits.
D. Minimal: align THINGS-EEG2 call shape only, defer. YAGNI, leaves heterogeneity unsolved.

Recommend A as the honest end-state. SCOPE: its own focused refactor PR (base.py + 2 existing adapters + harness + migrate things_eeg2), NOT bundled into the Stage-3 feature PR #21. Migrate things_eeg2 into the common interface as part of this.
close_reason: Audited: the labels-as-nullable-meta pattern (Option A) is ALREADY implemented at the STORE layer — store.load folds adapter y into meta['label_id']+['label'] (store.py:46), META_FIELDS carries them, and the harness/CV read label from meta, not positional y. So the class paradigm already uses the recommended pattern; retrieval (THINGS-EEG2) carries its analog (concept/image keys) via its own get_epochs. The only residue is the adapters' positional y at the get_data boundary, which the store immediately normalizes — a thin redundancy, not a missing abstraction. MIGRATION PATH (deferred): to drop positional y, change DatasetAdapter.get_data 3 impls (Moabb/Shin/braindecode) + store.load to return/read (X, meta-with-label_id); + extend META_FIELDS with nullable concept/stimulus_id and flow THINGS-EEG2 through the store. NOT done now: bounded-but-breaking refactor of the decode pipeline (Phases 1-2 depend on it) for marginal benefit, and full cross-paradigm generality (text/seq2seq) is speculative until a 3rd label type is real (no-speculative-interfaces). Revisit when that lands.
