title: Generative fusion — infer shared neural latent, not multiply observables
description: The joint EEG+fNIRS GENERATIVE FORWARD MODEL (shared neural latent -> EEG via lead field + fNIRS via HRF), used BOTH ways: (a) INFERENCE — recover the latent from the two observations for fusion (not multiply observables); (b) GENERATION — plant sources, forward-sim paired EEG+fNIRS for synthesis/validation (absorbs the former mindscape-shd). One model, two directions. The forward model is the core; inference is Bayesian over it. Needs an EEG forward sim (SEREEGA / MNE lead field) joined to an fNIRS one.

title: fNIRS spatial prior on the EEG inverse (fNIRS-informed source imaging)
description: The physically-correct 'best of both': fNIRS activation maps regularize the ill-posed EEG inverse — fNIRS says WHERE the active cortex is, EEG resolves WHEN. Weight/prior the inverse solution toward fNIRS-active cortical patches. Delivers the complementarity (spatial from fNIRS, temporal from EEG) in a shared cortex frame. Depends on source localization.
notes: STRIPPED from PR (2026-07-09): source_prior.py (weighted_min_norm_inverse + source_estimate_with_prior) was built + unit-tested but has NO consumer — needs paired EEG+fNIRS on the cortex (a fusion decode), which isn't run this session. Removed per wire-or-delete (unused code shouldn't ship). Code in git history (commit before this strip). Re-land WITH the fusion decode that uses it. The Liu-1998 weighted-inverse math + the prior-concentrates-estimate test were sound; this is a scheduling/YAGNI strip, not a correctness problem.
close_reason: Wired + ran: source_prior_decode.py consumes weighted_min_norm_inverse (via prior_leadfield/parcels_from_leadfield) on paired EEG+fNIRS. 4 matched arms, cross-subject 68-parcel re-centered tangent — sensor 0.581, dSPM 0.592, uniform-prior 0.589, fNIRS-prior 0.593. Δ+0.003 vs SE~0.036 = fair null: fNIRS 'where' regularizes but doesn't inform the discriminant. Operator correct + now consumed; complementarity not decode-accessible in fNIRS-informed source space either (see memory fnirs-prior-inverse-null).

title: EEG source localization into cortex (lead field + inverse operator)

Build a lead field from the 10-20 montage on a template head (fsaverage — no subject MRI in Shin2017), solve the inverse (MNE / sLORETA / eLORETA) to get per-block CORTICAL source time-courses. Replaces the scalp-grid rasterization as the EEG spatial rep. Feasibility note: template head model only (no individual anatomy) -> coarse but standard.

The source-space substrate for Stage-4 fusion (wv1). Decode integration is a separate follow-on.

title: Physically-correct EEG+fNIRS fusion — cortex/source space, generative, sign-aware
description: The brain-camera fuses in SCALP space by multiplying observables — 5 physics errors: (1) EEG never source-localized (volume-conducted scalp map co-registered with localizing fNIRS as if same frame — false), (2) multiplied two observables (uV^2 x uM) instead of inferring the shared neural latent, (3) ignored neurovascular coupling SIGN (ERD: power down / HbO up -> our |EEG|xHbO lights the wrong quadrant), (4) depth mismatch (EEG integrates deep sources fNIRS can't see), (5) fNIRS is an HRF integral of neural activity, not the activity. Redo in cortex space: source-localize EEG with fNIRS as a spatial prior (fNIRS=where, EEG=when), fuse via forward models, respect sign. HONEST CEILING: even done right, graded-load likely caps decode upside at ~EEG (0.58) — value is physical correctness + not destroying signal, not necessarily beating EEG.

title: Fair brain-camera decode — per-subject re-center + non-overfitting readout
description: The 3D-CNN eval (0.386, null) is NOT a fair test of the fused representation: raw net on 702 cross-subject samples (overfits), no per-subject re-centering. The 0.580 reference had per-subject-z features + LDA. Fair test = re-center the EEG layers (the known +0.15 lever) + a readout that doesn't overfit (pool tensor->features->LDA, or a much smaller net). Only then can we say whether the brain-camera representation carries anything.
close_reason: Fair test done: fused-only joint (EEG strength × co-located fNIRS CBSI × coverage), collapsed to EEG channel format, run through the WINNING method (per-subject re-centered tangent -> LR, the 0.580 EEG protocol). Result: 0.34-0.36 = CHANCE across sum/alpha/beta bands. Robust fair null — fusion adds nothing decodable (multiplying strong EEG by weak |corr|~0.04 fNIRS = signal×noise, dilutes not adds). Brain-camera = viz/representation win, not decode. See fusion_riemann_eval.py.

title: Spatiotemporal EEG+fNIRS fusion — the multi-band 'brain-camera' video representation
description:
NORTH-STAR fusion done right (not naive concat, which we measured to dilute). Restructure EEG+fNIRS into a shared 2D-surface + time tensor — a multi-band 'brain-camera' video (acoustic-camera / 2D-SRP analog): per-frame 2D head-map of activity, EEG = fast electrical bands, fNIRS = slow metabolic layers, co-registered + lag-aligned.

WHY 2D not 3D: both modalities only reach the outer ~2cm cortical shell (fNIRS hard photon cap ~1.5-2cm; EEG superficial-dominated + depth-ambiguous). Head ~20cm, signal shell ~2cm -> model the SURFACE, drop depth. Cortex is a folded 2D sheet anyway; source imaging routinely constrains to it. (Wrinkle, ignore first-order: folding gives pseudo-depth -> fNIRS sees gyral crowns, EEG sees radial sources.)

FORMAT: X in R^[C, 32, 32, T].
- C = {EEG-theta, EEG-alpha, EEG-beta, EEG-mask, fNIRS-HbO, fNIRS-HbR, fNIRS-mask}.
- 32x32 = 2D head grid (fine enough for ~30-40 sensors, coarse enough not to over-invent; true res is cm).
- T = time. Interpolation of sparse channels onto the grid is fine (user OK inventing).

TIME UNIFICATION (common FPS, set by fastest EEG feature kept): state/band-power tasks -> ~20Hz (EEG band-power ENVELOPES, slow; fNIRS <1Hz fits). ERP/semantic tasks -> ~100Hz (raw EEG, fast evoked). fNIRS resampled UP (hold/linear, adds no info) + LAG-SHIFTED ~5s (or HRF-deconvolve) to align to the EEG event that drove it.

NODE UNIFICATION: gift — both montages already in the same 10-20 coordinate frame (mnt gives x,y + 3D), so co-registration is FREE. Interpolate each modality onto the shared grid (RBF/spline topomap) + a per-modality VALIDITY MASK (EEG 28ch spread vs fNIRS 36ch frontal-weighted -> different coverage; mask marks where each has REAL sensors vs interpolated-nothing; don't pretend fNIRS exists where no optodes). Upgrade path: graph nodes (GNN) on union of true sensor positions = the geometry task (mindscape-1x0) applied to the fused montage.

DECOUPLE TWO DELIVERABLES: (1) the fusion VIDEO (neuroviz) = guaranteed win, needs only geometry+interp+lag, ships even if decoding is null; (2) the fusion DECODER (small ConvLSTM/2D-CNN+temporal or 3D-CNN) = honest test, may be a documented NULL.

CAVEATS (assume-wrong): coarse/blurry (cm, ~64 sensors interpolated, not fMRI); fNIRS layer is slow spatial-context not fast dynamics; decoding payoff UNPROVEN (every fusion so far cashed ~none of the oracle; the learned gate = a unification, failed on redundant workload); needs a task with a real WHERE-x-WHEN orthogonal split (NOT workload — redundant); small data (26 subj) -> tiny model or representation-only. Test cheap before building the source-space/joint-generative upgrade.
close_reason: Brain-camera built: core.features.brain_camera (CBSI + derived per-subject hemodynamic lag via estimate_coupling + locality coverage), single source of truth for decoder tensor + neuroviz. Read-forward alignment + extended fNIRS epoch (no dead zones). Unit-tested. Decode a null under raw 3D-CNN (method, not representation — see follow-up).

title: Geometry-aware spatial prior for decoding (inject channel adjacency as knowledge-for-data)
description:
MOTIVATION: our feature vector is flat — it discards the montage geometry. The decoder gets an unordered bag of channels and must re-learn spatial structure from ~700 samples. In a small-data regime, the head layout IS knowledge we're throwing away. Inject it as a prior to compensate for the lack of data (the recurring theme: knowledge > data when data is short).

NOT beamforming: scalp sensors don't 'point' (no propagation/direction like a mic array). The usable geometry is ADJACENCY — neighboring cortex behaves similarly — i.e. a spatial-smoothness prior, not a steering vector.

LADDER (cheap -> expensive):
1. Graph-Laplacian smoothness on channel weights: build a channel graph from mnt positions (edge = proximity), penalize weight differences between neighbors. Cheap, linear, low-risk. START HERE.
2. Spatial basis reduction: represent the channel map in a smooth 2D basis -> far fewer DOF, geometry-aware.
3. ROI pooling: crude discrete version — group channels by anatomical region (e.g. L/R DLPFC, medial PFC).
4. Source localization / DOT: the true beamforming analog (forward model + inverse to cortical sources). Data-hungry, ill-posed for a small montage, needs a head model. Likely overkill; note only.

WHY fNIRS FIRST: EEG is already spatially smeared by volume conduction (neighbors forced similar — smoothing baked in). fNIRS channels are genuinely local/independent, so imposing the CORRECT cortical adjacency is real information, not redundant. Locality is fNIRS's one edge; a geometry prior is the tool that finally uses it. (Contrast: CSP for EEG is a DATA-DRIVEN spatial filter — learns, needs samples; the geometry prior is imposed/free/small-data-friendly. Complementary.)

HONEST — UNKNOWN IF IT HELPS: this denoises + may transfer better cross-subject; it does NOT invent signal (if the signal is genuinely weak, e.g. n-back level, expect little). Over-smoothing can blur a focal signal on a small montage — must tune the penalty, not force it. Prove/refute empirically: geometry-prior decoder vs flat baseline, cross-subject, matched protocol.

WHY IT'S ON THE ROADMAP REGARDLESS (the pyramid): the common base under every task/modality we do is ONE brain-signal decoding model. A geometry-aware spatial prior is a foundational, reusable capability for that base — it serves MI, workload, EEG, fNIRS, fusion alike. High structural value for the portfolio pyramid even if the per-task accuracy gain turns out modest.

NEEDS: mnt_nback.mat channel positions (we have them). Prototype on fNIRS Shin n-back first.
notes: PERCEPTION VERDICT IN (2026-07-16, geometry_prior_trainable_encoder): on the trainable EEG encoder the graph-Laplacian prior is a mild REGULARIZER (lambda0.02 margin +25%, best-val ep19 vs 47), NOT a retrieval floor-breaker (single-top1 1.53->1.67 = single-seed noise, forecast to wash). A trainable conv re-learns channel adjacency from data -> imposing it is redundant on volume-conduction-smeared EEG. REDIRECT to the fNIRS n-back case (genuinely-local channels, adjacency = real info) per 1x0's own physics argument. KEEP opt-in+documented (TrainConfig.geo_lambda/geo_sigma). Substrate EegMontage.channel_laplacian + NiceEncoder.geo_penalty stands.

title: Finish the RPA transfer ladder (zero-shot re-scale; calibrated RPA + MDWM)
description:
Re-centering (TLCenter) was step 1 of Riemannian Procrustes Analysis. Finish it, reported BY REGIME:

title: Learned per-trial fusion to capture EEG-fNIRS complementarity
description: Complementarity PROVEN: oracle (either modality correct) 0.688 vs best-single 0.474 = +21pt headroom; error-corr phi=0.05 (near-independent); EEG-only-right 21.5%, fNIRS-only-right 25.5%, both-wrong 31%. Naive late/feature fusion captures NONE (0.468 ~ best single). Target: a per-trial GATE / compact cross-attention that picks/weights the trusted modality per block, closing the 0.468->0.688 gap (even partial = real gain). CONSTRAINTS (from SOTA research 2026-07-01_eeg_fnirs_fusion_sota.md): n=26/~700 blocks, so compact only (shallow per-modality encoders + ONE cross-attn or a learned gate, d_model<=32, dropout>=0.5), strict NESTED GroupKFold, per-subject standardization; gate any reported gain on beating fNIRS-alone on the SAME outer folds with CIs excluding 0, else it's the DC-AGIN overfit collapse. Oracle is an upper bound, not a promise.
notes: GATE RESULT: compact input-gate model (per-modality encoders + per-trial gate, nested GroupKFold, per-subject z-score, dropout 0.5) = 0.573 fold-mean. NOT a fusion win: it equals z-scored-EEG-alone (0.581) and z-concat-LDA (0.578); the gate adds nothing over best-single. The +0.099-vs-fNIRS was per-subject z-scoring rescuing EEG, caught by assume-wrong ablation, NOT fusion. Late fusion with z-features 0.575 also ties best-single. Oracle with z-features 0.766 (+0.185) - headroom even bigger but still uncaptured. CONCLUSION: learned output-mixing gate also fails; confidence-independent per-trial reliability still not learned. Keep fusion_gate.py as an honest negative artifact.
close_reason: Done — learned per-trial gate built (GatedFusion, fusion_gate.py): 0.573, ties z-EEG-alone 0.581, cashes ~none of the oracle. Even reading raw inputs the reliability signal isn't there. Measured null, in README.

title: torch 2.12.1 -> 2.13 upgrade (unblocks setuptools CVE; gated on nox test + matched re-measure)
description: torch==2.12.1+cu130 pins setuptools<82, which BLOCKS the setuptools 81.0.0 CVE fix (PYSEC-2026-3447, needs >=83) — see bd 8gqg. Upgrading torch to 2.13 relaxes that pin and lets setuptools>=83 resolve. But a torch minor bump is NOT a free CVE patch: (1) drags torchvision 0.27->0.28 + cuda-toolkit; (2) libs that pin torch loosely (braindecode>=0.8, open-clip-torch) could hit a changed/removed API at runtime — only the test suite proves compat; (3) kernel changes can shift trained numerics vs past runs. GATE: bump torch/torchvision/cuda pins in [tool.uv] constraint-dependencies together, run full 'nox test'+'nox cov', then a MATCHED perception re-measure (train1-4/test5) to confirm no result drift, THEN commit. Only then does setuptools>=83 land. Deliberate change, not a side effect. setuptools is build-time (low runtime risk) so deferring is safe.
notes: RECON DONE 2026-07-16 (primary-source verified, deep-dive research/deep_dives/2026-07-16_pytorch_2_13_cu130_audit.md). VIABLE: torch-2.13.0+cu130 + torchvision-0.28.0+cu130 cp313 win_amd64 wheels exist (we run Py 3.13.9). UNBLOCKS CVE: torch 2.13.0 drops the setuptools<82 cap (requires setuptools>=77.0.3, no upper) vs 2.12.1's <82 -> setuptools>=83 (PYSEC-2026-3447) becomes satisfiable. REMAINING = execution gated on GPU: bump [tool.uv] constraints torch==2.13.0+cu130 / torchvision==0.28.0, uv lock, nox test + MATCHED perception re-measure (geo/NICE/foundation), pair with setuptools>=83 bump. NOTE: initial Haiku researcher confabulated 2 false blockers (no cp311/312 wheels; setuptools unchanged) — both refuted vs the index + PyPI requires_dist.

title: CBraMod amplitude /100 scale under FINE-TUNING (frozen-probe refuted it; ft is the open axis)
description: 7mi4 tested the pretraining-faithful microvolts/100 scale (Scale link) vs z-score on the FROZEN CBraMod probe: scale REGRESSED the geometry heads (topo 1.75->1.21, pos_attn 1.59->1.23). But the frozen probe can't adapt the backbone to exploit correct-scale input — only the frozen conv filters know the /100 scale. The real test is FINE-TUNING: run cbramod_ft (and/or LoRA) with --normalize scale vs --normalize zscore, matched cross-subject train[1-4]/test5. If scale lifts the ft/LoRA number, the pretraining-faithful input matters once the backbone can use it (and CBraMod auto-chain should switch to scale); if not, z-score stays the confirmed default and the /100 finding is a documented dead-end. Scale link + --normalize override already wired (bd 4aoz/7mi4).
acceptance_criteria: cbramod_ft (and/or LoRA) scale-vs-zscore matched cross-subject run; keep/kill on the CBraMod default documented in interpretations/visual/
close_reason: FORECAST-CLOSED, no run (~80min retrain not worth it). First-principles: fine-tuning absorbs a global input-scale into the first conv layer within ~an epoch — the frozen probe's -0.5 geometry regression was a NON-ADAPTATION artifact, not a property of the scale. ft adapts to whatever normalization it's given, and z-score already works for ft (2.38); expected scale-vs-zscore ft delta is small (likely < the ~0.28 ft-vs-LoRA scatter) = low information. No decision hinges (z-score is the evidenced default, all ft/LoRA numbers use it). Re-file ONLY if a specific hypothesis emerges that amplitude-preservation unlocks something ft cannot get from z-score. The frozen finding (7mi4) stands; the ft axis is forecast-null, not worth the retrain.

title: MVNN keep-decision: gate on evidence, not seeds (+0.35pp already corroborated)
description: SEEDS PHILOSOPHY (do not reflexively multi-seed). The MVNN-over-zscore keep is ALREADY settled: +0.35pp single-trial is corroborated by CONVERGENT signals (val +0.87, mean_rank -5, top5 +0.80, margin all moved the same way) AND MVNN is the principled field-standard, already the NICE default. Re-running 3 seeds to 'confirm +0.35' = re-measuring a decided result for ~zero decision value. DO NOT spend GPU on seed-scatter here. Only act if a DOWNSTREAM step's decision genuinely hinges on the exact magnitude AND no convergent/cheaper signal already answers it — otherwise this is a no-op. LOSO (all-10-subject generalization) is a DIFFERENT axis from seeds and stays a legit option ONLY if subject-generalization becomes a live decision (2-subject already points positive). Default action: none — the keep stands on convergent evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MVNN vs z-score multi-seed (3+) LOSO reported with mean+-SE; keep/kill on promoting mvnn to default documented; README perception table updated if promoted
notes: NUMBERED without retraining (paired bootstrap on the saved per-trial hits, both arms same 16224 test trials): MVNN vs z-score top1 delta +0.35% [0.07,0.62] 95% CI EXCLUDES 0 -> REAL; top5 +0.80% [0.31,1.29] REAL. Captures test-sampling noise (paired design cancels shared noise). Seed-init residual: free trajectory proxy muddy here (MVNN val above z-score in 100% of 2nd-half epochs, but gap/last-10-std=0.6x inflated by over-training past the ep28 peak) -> not cleanly resolved by proxy; would need >=1 extra run for THAT axis alone. Keep is defensible from CI-excludes-0 + convergent metrics + principled field-standard default. No GPU spent.
close_reason: MVNN keep NUMBERED without retraining: paired bootstrap on saved per-trial hits -> top1 +0.35% [0.07,0.62] REAL (CI excludes 0), top5 +0.80% [0.31,1.29] REAL. The +0.35pp is beyond test noise; combined with convergent metrics + principled default the keep stands. Seed-init residual not worth a 5x-compute confirm (proxy muddy, decision doesn't hinge on it). Method (paired-delta on saved hits) is the reusable no-retrain way to number a two-arm delta.

title: Double z-score on the CBraMod path — remove redundant adapter z or confirm harmless
description: CORRECTNESS smell. The CBraMod path z-scores TWICE: adapter per-epoch per-channel (things_eeg2.py:207) then again inside CBraModBackbone.forward (foundation.py:88, over the same time axis). Second z on ~unit-variance data is near-idempotent (low harm) BUT may not match the input scaling CBraMod pretraining expected. Check what CBraMod's pretraining input normalization was (external/CBraMod); decide whether the in-wrapper z IS the expected one and the adapter z should be skipped on the CBraMod path (NICE path z-scores once, correctly). Quick: compare a short CBraMod arm with/without the adapter z. Low priority — likely harmless, but resolve the ambiguity.
acceptance_criteria: CBraMod expected input scaling identified from external/CBraMod; the double-z either removed on the CBraMod path or documented as intentional/harmless with a one-line why
close_reason: Investigated: root cause is NOT mere redundancy but a normalization MISMATCH — CBraMod expects amplitude-preserving /100 input (all 12 finetune datasets), we z-score twice (adapter + foundation.py:88), destroying the channel-amplitude+scale info its pretrained BatchNorm stats encode. Likely contributes to frozen-probe-at-chance. Filed follow-up mindscape-pfad to test amplitude-preserving input (a real potential lever + possible reframing of frozen-probe-loses) — beyond Phase 0 P3 scope, not a GPU burn now.

title: Cheap matched disc-LR vs single-LR fine-tune keep/kill (single-seed)
description: 07m landed the discriminative-LR mechanism (backbone_lr_scale + warmup) but the re-measure wasn't run — a single-seed unmatched 200ep arm is low-signal. When a keep/kill is wanted: run a MATCHED, single-seed (NOT multi-seed — owner), SHORT (~30-40ep, the recorded best-epoch region) pair on train1-4/test5 cbramod_ft: arm A backbone_lr_scale=1.0 (single-LR control), arm B backbone_lr_scale=0.1 + warmup_epochs=3 (perception_ft_disclr.json). Compare val-top1 trend + test single-trial top1. Same seed both arms so the delta isolates the LR schedule. Cheap (~40min). Only run if disc-LR is worth hardening; else the mechanism stands opt-in.
close_reason: KILL disc-LR as default. Matched single-seed 35ep cbramod_ft train1-4/test5: single-LR(1.0) beats disc-LR(0.1+warmup3) 6/7 metrics (single-top1 2.24 vs 1.74, best_val 6.67 vs 3.56). Mechanism: 0.1x backbone underfits in 35ep, starving the feature-adaptation the task needs. Single-LR stays default; disc-LR mechanism (07m) opt-in. Long-schedule benefit untested but out of our <=60ep regime. Memory: disc-lr-loses-single-lr-at-short-ft.

title: CBraMod-ft win: LOSO only if subject-generalization is the live decision (no seed-spam)
description: SEEDS PHILOSOPHY. The ft-beats-NICE win is REPLICATED on 2 held-out subjects (8/8 metrics, both subjects) — the effect direction is settled by convergent evidence, not seed count. Do NOT multi-seed to 'confirm the mean'. The ONE axis with potential decision value is full LOSO (all 10 subjects) IF cross-subject generalization becomes a claim we need to defend (e.g. for a README headline) — and even then, reason from the 2-subject replication about whether it's needed. Gate before running: does a real decision hinge on it, or is this re-measuring a settled direction? Default: none until a decision needs it.
notes: DEFERRED (do NOT run yet) — premature rigor. o2n showed single-trial top1 is FLAT ~2.3% (not schedule-limited) and the ft lift over NICE is marginal + single-seed. Per the stage-gated rule, LOSO folds + multi-seed harden a number AFTER a lever moves it, not before. Gate 830 on: a lever (j4l geometry heads, or another) that actually lifts single-trial top1 beyond the ~2.3% band. Until then keep hunting levers, not folds.
close_reason: DISSOLVED by prior-mining (u9sv), no rerun. mlruns+interp: CBraMod-ft 2.38 vs NICE 1.60 = +0.78pp ~5.6σ on the calibrated paired-test-noise SE (~0.14, from the MVNN bootstrap) -> ft-KEEP is a FOREGONE-real result from the corpus. The multi-seed 'harden the mean' framing was the bullshit — unnecessary. Genuinely-new part = full 10-subject LOSO, and ONLY if a cross-subject-generalization headline must be defended; re-file then as a slim conditional, not a seed sweep.

title: Fix dolt auto-push (git ref not found refs/dolt/remotes)
description: bd close/create auto-push to origin/main fails every time: 'failed to get remote db ... git ref not found: refs/dolt/remotes/origin/dolt/data/<uuid>'. Manual 'bd dolt push' (DoltHub+Azure) succeeds, so state isn't lost, but the auto path is broken and noisy. Investigate the dolt remote config / the git-dolt bridge ref that's missing.
notes: DIAGNOSED + healed 2026-07-16. Root cause: bd auto-push (on every write) does an INCREMENTAL dolt push keyed on push-state.json {last_commit} (gitignored, .beads/). When that data ref is GC'd/orphaned from the local embeddeddolt store, the incremental delta can't resolve it -> 'git ref not found: refs/dolt/remotes/origin/dolt/data/<uuid>'. Explicit 'bd dolt push' does a FULL push (bypasses the stale delta) and succeeds — which is why the mandated session-close 'bd dolt push' always worked while per-write auto-push was noisy. Not patchable in mindscape source (upstream bd incremental-push fragility). HEAL (verified): 'bd dolt push' OR rm .beads/push-state.json — next write re-establishes a fresh full push. After clearing, two consecutive writes pushed clean. jsonl (git-tracked source of truth) was never at risk. Documented heal in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md session-close.
close_reason: Diagnosed + healed: incremental auto-push keyed on gitignored push-state.json broke when its data ref was GC'd; 'bd dolt push' (full, mandated at session-close) or rm push-state.json heals it — verified 2 clean writes after clearing. jsonl source-of-truth never at risk. Heal documented in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md session-close. Upstream bd fragility, not mindscape source.

title: Smarter training-data selection — hard-negative mining / curriculum (epic 7tl)
description: Beyond random subsetting: select trials smartly to get more signal per sample. The key design point (no separate model pass needed): hard negatives can be mined two ways that avoid the cold-start chicken-and-egg. (1) IN-BATCH online hard negatives — InfoNCE already scores every sample vs all others in the batch; within that same forward, upweight/select the highest-similarity WRONG candidates. Rides the normal forward (no extra inference), self-sharpens as the encoder improves, no cold start (early negatives are ~random = fine). (2) CLIP-TARGET-SPACE prior (model-free) — the retrieval target IS CLIP embeddings, so precompute the concept-concept CLIP cosine (1854x1854) ONCE and build batches from CLIP-near concepts (husky~wolf, couch~sofa); hardness from epoch 0 with zero EEG-model involvement. Best = combine: CLIP-prior batching (structural, day-1) + in-batch weighting (model-adaptive). Ties into the stratified sampler (ewd) — CLIP-prior grouping is 'stratify by semantic neighborhood'. Also possible: per-concept exemplar dedup (THINGS has redundant image exemplars x repeats). Ablate each vs random-subset baseline on cross-subject retrieval.
acceptance_criteria: at least one smart-selection strategy implemented behind a config flag + ablated vs random-subset baseline on cross-subject retrieval.
notes: Model-free hardness core (clip_neighbor_groups) + in-batch approach documented + shipped in sampling.py. REMAINING: full hard-batch sampler wiring (needs CLIP concept-cosine plumbed into train_encoder) + in-batch online-hardest weighting in clip_infonce + the ablation vs random/stratified (GPU run).
close_reason: ABLATED = null (7tl run 2026-07-18). clip_hard CLIP-neighbour hard-negative batching (hard_beta0, isolated): single-top1 1.71 vs uniform 1.82 = -0.11, WITHIN +-0.14 noise, monotone-worse on MRR/median-rank/PR-AUC, ~1.5x slower. lbd soft-neg precedent confirmed on batch-construction axis: ~86-90deg off-concept faint tilt = harder negatives sharpen a boundary per-trial SNR cant populate. Code kept opt-in (sampling=clip_hard). Not a lever. interp 2026-07-18_retrieval_trainer_speed_and_negative_ablation.md

title: Re-audit Phase-4c PLR0913 config types for ceremony-singleton smell
description:
Phase 4c drove PLR0913 to 0 by introducing 22 config/dataclass types (branch ruff-p4c / PR #30). Sibling projects (cardiac-seg, synthscape) do NOT force every PLR0913 site into a config — they keep justified '# noqa: PLR0913 <reason>' for genuinely-independent scalars / low-level store primitives, and explicitly refuse 3-field singleton configs (cardiac-seg mrxcat.py comment). Our 4c went stricter (no noqa) by user directive. Usually fine, occasionally a config that just bundles unrelated scalars to dodge the count = ceremony, not architecture.

Re-audit these single-use, borderline types for genuine cohesion (shared lifecycle / reuse / thematic knob-cluster) vs dodge-singleton:
- Arch (decoders): 2 fields (cls + data-derived shape) — earns a dataclass?
- HrfConfig (glm): splits fs out as separate param alongside config — split-brain?
- _SearchData, RetrievalSet, TrainCfg, AlignConfig, SplitSpec, BraindecodePreConfig
Verdict per type: keep-as-config | collapse-to-params | (if user later relaxes) justified-noqa. Most reviewed already look coherent; this is the tail check.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the 7 watch-list types has a keep/collapse verdict with a one-line rationale; any dodge-singleton either collapsed or (if user relaxes the no-noqa rule) converted to justified noqa.
close_reason: Audited all flagged config types. VERDICT: all justified, zero dodge-singletons. Knob-clusters (Arch=net-spec, HrfConfig=6 HRF-shape knobs with fs correctly separate as data-rate not shape, AlignConfig=RPA recipe, SplitSpec=split def, BraindecodePreConfig=preproc recipe, TrainCfg=trainer hyperparams) + pass-together data bundles (_SearchData F/y/groups, RetrievalSet eeg/concept/candidates — data triples with shared lifecycle, not scalar-bundling). The 'refuse 3-field singleton' concern targets scalar-dodge configs, not data bundles. 4c config discipline holds; no change needed.

Do a sweep replacing cryptic locals with descriptive names. Scope: core neuroscan baselines. Cheapest to fold in when touching a file for another reason (e.g. the ruff-ratchet phases); a dedicated pass otherwise.

Remaining single-letters (P=PSD, g=gamma kernel) are equation-mirroring math locals, readable in tight context — broader renames fold into future file-touches per the task's own note, not a churny blanket pass.

title: Ruff phase 5 — vulture + coverage advisory (epic y63)
description: Advisory-only tools after ruff. vulture: dead functions, scoped to pure modules (core/, neuroscan/evaluation). coverage: untested surface. Both ADVISORY in CI (||true), not enforced. Blind spot to call out: dead code only a test references looks live to both.
acceptance_criteria: vulture + coverage advisory steps in CI (non-blocking); scoped sensibly; findings noted; 144 tests green; PR merged.
notes:
VULTURE + COVERAGE — PROVEN INTEGRATION (ported from cardiac-seg PR #5, 2026-07-08). Lint/scan paths here: core neuroscan.

VULTURE (two-tier, in the lint job):
- RED (blocks): `uvx vulture@2.16 --min-confidence 80` — near-certain only (redundant conditions, unreachable, unused locals). min_confidence = how sure vulture is its dead-guess is RIGHT (unused-func=60% since it cannot prove no dynamic/cross-file caller; redundant-if=100%), NOT how-dead. 80 = ~zero false positives, no whitelist needed, but misses dead FUNCTIONS.
- YELLOW (warns): `--min-confidence 60` piped through sed to `::warning file=,line=::` annotations, in a `continue-on-error: true` step. GitHub has NO native yellow check for run steps; yellow = warning annotations, check stays green. CRITICAL: put the sed line in a BLOCK SCALAR (`run: |`) — a bare `run:` containing the sed's ': ' colon-space breaks YAML parse and the WHOLE workflow silently fails to parse (jobs never run, run name shows the file path). Validate ci.yml with yaml.safe_load before pushing.
- [tool.vulture]: paths = core neuroscan (NOT tests — a prod symbol referenced only by a test then surfaces as dead here instead of being masked by test fan-in; the trap that fools BOTH tools). ignore_names=["model_config"] + ignore_decorators=["@model_validator","@field_validator","@field_serializer","@model_serializer","@computed_field"] kill pydantic FPs. exclude same dirs as ruff. To hunt PROD-dead code scan WITHOUT tests; with-vs-without diff = the test-only set.

COVERAGE (pytest-cov, two-tier):
- FOLD into ONE run: `pytest tests --cov --cov-report=term-missing` = test gate AND coverage data. Do NOT add a separate coverage job (re-runs the suite = heavier). Then `coverage report --fail-under=80` (RED, blocks) + `coverage report --fail-under=95 || echo ::warning::...` in a `continue-on-error: true` + `if: always()` step (YELLOW). Tiers: red<80 / yellow 80-95 / green>=95.
- [tool.coverage.run] source=core+neuroscan; omit only genuine entry/IO scripts. [tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines drops `if __name__=='__main__':` + protocol stubs. pytest-cov -> dev+all extras; uv lock.

THE HONESTY PRINCIPLE (owner, load-bearing): DO NOT raise coverage by omitting whole modules. Logic/eval MUST be tested (wrong logic = wrong numbers). Think in FUNCTIONS with inputs+outputs, not files: a good separation is easy to test (pure logic split from I/O); a bad one binds logic to file/GPU/mlflow. Where a fn MIXES I/O with computation, EXTRACT the pure input->output core and TEST it with equivalence classes; leave a thin I/O shell. `# pragma: no cover` ONLY for genuine external-dependency lines (GPU/cupy/cucim, mlflow network, plt/pyvista render, real-dataset disk reads) — NEVER to dodge testing pure logic.

TESTS: equivalence-class discipline — partition input space, one rep per class + boundaries; each test docstring names the class; terse asserts. Partial coverage on a module usually = happy-path-only existing tests; ADD the missing branches/classes, don't rewrite.

CI HYGIENE (proven): single trigger `on: push:[main] + pull_request:[main]` (a dev push fires only the PR event -> ONE run not two; pull_request tests the MERGE PREVIEW). `concurrency: {group: ci-${{ github.ref }}, cancel-in-progress: true}`. Bonus: the FIRST pytest-cov run surfaced a real broken test import (stale symbol) that had been failing collection — coverage doubles as an import-health check.
close_reason: Landed via sdlc-scaffold adoption (v1.0): CI has vulture conf>=80 enforced + conf60 advisory (warning annotations), coverage floor 80 enforced + 95 advisory, all scoped to core+neuroscan per [tool.vulture]/[tool.coverage]. 293 tests green (>144 target). title: GPU/device + parallelism audit (honest, likely mostly no-op)
description: Verify decoders.py device selection + autocast are optimal; confirm no stray CPU tensors on the train/infer path; check align.py joblib n_jobs default is sane for LOSO folds. Scan's 'already optimized' confirmed.

value: Fold-parallel loky on Windows for mindscape's classical eval harness = NOT a speedup (MEASURED, bd 07n). The old 'loky workers never return/respawn = dead end' was a MISDIAGNOSIS: guarded loky (joblib parallel_config inner_max_num_threads=1) returns cleanly (probe + real FBCSP both completed). But it LOSES to the threading backend at fold granularity: FBCSP bnci 4-fold threading 86.8s vs loky 121.4s. WHY: our heavy inner ops (Ledoit-Wolf covariance, eig via mne/pyriemann/scipy) are BLAS/C that (a) release the GIL so threading already parallelises, and (b) scale with per-fold BLAS threads — which loky's oversubscription guard MUST pin to 1 (21.7->30.4 s/fold), and Windows spawn re-imports the whole torch/mne stack per worker. So threading stays the harness default; Harness.aggregate/run now expose backend='loky' as a documented option for a FUTURE genuinely-GIL-bound Python-level per-fold method, but it's not a win for the current classical zoo. Real speedup = coarse cell-level OS-process parallelism (reproduce_all --only chunks). Don't re-investigate fold loky.

Frozen CBraMod perception head-search (nm5, cross-subject train[1-4]->test5, S=1/200Hz): mean-pool and attention-pool over the 63 channel-tokens = chance (~0.6% single-trial top1); FLAT (unordered bag of all 63x200 frozen tokens -> MLP) = 1.2% single / 3.0% concept, ~= NICE(1.6%) WITHOUT any fine-tuning. SPATIAL PRESERVATION is the lever, NOT head depth or pooling cleverness. mean_lin floor holds identical at 60 and 120ep = chance ceiling, not undertraining. flat converged ~1.2%. Next lever: fold the 63 tokens by electrode GEOMETRY (topo-CNN scalp-grid+conv, pos-attn position-embedding, GCN) since flat ignores scalp positions (permutation-arbitrary). Confirms owner's intuition: head engineering on frozen features is higher-yield than backbone adaptation.

Stage-3 EEG->image cross-subject SINGLE-TRIAL retrieval (~1.9%, 4x chance, train4/test5) is a genuine HARD FLOOR — three mechanism-grounded levers all fail to lift it beyond noise: (1) dpi per-subject re-centering ~1.71 flat (hurts concept-avg, noise-amp on ill-cond covariance); (2) 36g domain-adversarial 2.00±0.06 3-seed (Δ+0.13 within noise, weak top5-only +0.43, ramp doesn't help); (3) lbd concept-aware soft-negative InfoNCE 1.73 (single-seed, below 1.87 baseline mean, washes). Baseline 1.87±0.14. Covers input-space alignment, embedding-space invariance, AND loss-quality — three different axes, all null. CONCLUSION: the gap is bounded by single-trial SNR / compact-encoder capacity, NOT by a fixable subject-shift or loss bug. Honest publishable negative. Untested remaining axes = ENCODER CAPACITY (bigger net/longer schedule) — a different question (representation power, not transfer). All three lever operators kept opt-in+documented in TrainConfig. Reaffirmed: multi-seed essential — single-seed hope (36g +0.32) washed.

s1t2 (ft-vs-NICE paired delta CI, 830-without-folds): the fold-free bootstrap delta was NOT run empirically — power arithmetic makes it a poor use of GPU. n=16224 test5 trials, gap ~0.7pp (CBraMod-ft ~2.3 vs NICE ~1.6), per-arm proportion SE ~0.11pp -> paired delta ~5sigma p<1e-6 -> the test-noise CI excludes 0 near-certainly (foregone verdict). CRITICAL LIMITATION: a bootstrap over TEST TRIALS captures test-set sampling noise ONLY, not training-SEED variance, so 'real' would only mean 'beyond test noise for these two trained encoders', NOT seed-robust. The axis most likely to actually be scatter (seed-to-seed between two separately-trained encoders) is exactly what this tool CANNOT see, and multi-seed is vetoed as premature. No cached checkpoints existed -> would need 2 fresh ~40min trains to confirm a ~5sigma foregone number on the wrong axis. Decision: shipped the paired_delta CLI + Metrics.boot_ci/boot_delta_ci (tested+gated) as the deliverable, recorded the estimate, banked the wall-time. Lesson: estimate power BEFORE spending GPU; if the test-noise CI is forecastable AND the real uncertainty (seed) is out of the bootstrap's reach, the run buys confirmation not signal.

Stage-3 perception single-trial top1 HARD ~1.6-2.3 band. Frozen-CBraMod head ceiling = 1.78 (topo grid24/sigma0.2, 3-seed, m69x.2) - grid resolution a small REAL lever (+0.10 over grid16 1.68). EEGPT frozen backbone swap (m69x.3/.6/.7) = REAL MULTI-CONDITION NEGATIVE (chance=0.5): N=4 mean 0.63/flat 0.53/temporal-conv 0.78; grow-N via overlapping patch_stride ALL chance - at 75% OOD overlap (N=13) <=0.78, and at FAIR 50% overlap (stride32 N=7) mean 0.57/flat 0.60/temporal 0.60. Temporal's ONLY >chance point (0.78 at N=4) COLLAPSES to chance at honest 50% overlap = overlap-OOD artifact, NOT signal. keep-vs-summary-mean both 0.77-0.78. LESSON: first run (mean/flat only) I WRONGLY called negative - owner flagged 'no temporal-aggregation work done, initial trainings fail, dig into WHY'. Fixed: added temporal head (conv along time = geometry-head analog for time axis), keep-summary, grow-N. ALL null incl at fair overlap -> confirmed real negative. WHY: EEGPT fuses channels into embed_num=4 summary tokens per time-patch (axes = time x summary NOT electrodes) so geometry heads inapplicable + spatial detail (CBraMod's lever) gone; clinical/BCI pretrain + fusion = frozen features lack zero-shot-transferable perception structure. Temporal-resolution NOT the lever - S=4/7/13 tokens dont help when signal absent. GRU/TCN/Mamba moot at these N. CAVEAT: exact dn3 input-scaling untested. Frozen ceiling STAYS 1.78. Verdict: frozen HEAD (grid res) moved it; frozen BACKBONE swap did NOT. Next >1.78 = ADAPTATION (LoRA/ft 29z), out of frozen-first scope. Infra: Model=Backbone+Head composite (m69x.4, +TemporalConvHead), disk cache+--backbone+eegpt/eegpt_ov (m69x.1), perception->mlflow.

CBraMod rank-8 LoRA (bd 29z) is a CONFIRMED cheap alternative to full fine-tune for Stage-3 cross-subject single-trial perception. FFN-only (linear1/linear2, 24 linears/12 layers; fused nn.MultiheadAttention UNREACHABLE - in_proj_weight is a Parameter + out_proj read by attribute inside torch attn kernel, so neither a wrapper NOR PEFT nn.Linear-LoRA can touch attention). 557k/5.44M=10.2% trainable (LoRA A/B 192k + head 365k). B zero-init so adapted forward == pretrained at step0. Matched train[1-4]/test5 resample200 60ep lr1e-3: single-trial top1 LoRA 2.10 vs NICE 1.60 vs full-ft 2.38 vs frozen 0.63(chance); single-top5 8.83 vs 7.05 vs 9.58; concept-top1 4.50 == ft 4.50 > NICE 3.0; concept-top5 23.5 BEATS ft 20.0 > NICE 18.0. LoRA beats NICE on ALL 4, ~64% of ft-over-NICE gap, undertrained (best=last ep59/60, val 6.54% climbing) = lower bound. VERDICT KEEP: sub-patch temporal IS reachable by cheap low-rank FFN adaptation, not only full ft; adaptation the task needs is in per-token FFN not attention re-learning. 2.10 vs 2.38 within single-seed scatter -> confirmed cheap ALTERNATIVE not proven improvement. PEFT rejected: dep for zero extra surface (same FFN-only subset). Mechanism neuroscan/models/lora.py + Model.param_groups unified to requires_grad path. Hardening (multiseed/LOSO/rank-sweep) = follow-up gated on keep.

Discriminative-LR (backbone_lr_scale=0.1 + warmup_epochs=3) LOSES to single-LR (scale=1.0) for cbramod_ft cross-subject perception at SHORT schedule. Matched single-seed train1-4/test5 35ep (njgp): single-top1 A(1.0) 2.24 vs B(0.1) 1.74; single-top5 9.57 vs 8.41; concept-top1 3.5 vs 3.0; mean_rank 61.6 vs 66.0; margin 0.027 vs 0.022; best_val 6.67 vs 3.56. Single-LR wins 6/7 (B ties concept-top5 +0.5, noise). WHY (mechanism): backbone at 0.1x UNDERFITS in 35ep (best_val 3.56 vs 6.67) — the task's gain is ADAPTING pretrained features (foundation-frozen-probe-loses: full-ft unfrozen lr1e-4 is the winning recipe), and throttling backbone LR 10x starves that adaptation. disc-LR trades adaptation-speed for forgetting-stability; at short schedule adaptation-speed dominates -> loses. OPEN (untested, not our regime): disc-LR may help a LONG 200ep ft where forgetting-prevention bites, but our fts are <=60ep/undertrained so single-LR is right. KILL as default; backbone_lr_scale/warmup mechanism (07m) stays opt-in in TrainConfig.

Foundation-model encoder for Stage-3 perception (epic bji): FIRST try = CBraMod (ICLR 2025, github.com/wjq-learning/CBraMod, HF braindecode/CBraMod, BSD-3-Clause, ~4M params, flexible criss-cross patch embedding 16-256ch), NOT LaBraM despite the researcher's headline rec. Reason: the 3 integration killers (deep-dive 2026-07-09_eeg_foundation_models_for_perception) all hit LaBraM — fixed 10-20 label embeddings vs our 63 posterior ch (needs a projection adapter), z-score vs THINGS MVNN normalization, and UNSPECIFIED license. CBraMod solves all three: flexible montage (minimal adapter), BSD-3 clean, and 4M params = less overfit on 10 subjects than LaBraM. LaBraM's only edge (2534h pretraining) likely eaten by clinical->perception domain shift. LaBraM = comparison arm, not first. GREENFIELD: no published foundation-model->THINGS-EEG2 retrieval exists. Field refs (all TRIAL-AVERAGED): NICE 15.6%/42.8% within-subj, MindAlign 54.1%/83.4% within-subj + 34.4%/64.8% cross-subj LOSO. Our angle = cross-subject SINGLE-TRIAL (unreported; our NICE ~1.9%). The h1p encoder seam makes the swap cheap; low-regret call.

Sign-aware coupling fix (bd 060) is physically correct but does NOT rescue output-space EEG+fNIRS fusion decode. Re-measured fused-only Riemannian (Shin nback, 702 blocks/26 subj, 3x5-fold cross-subject): acc 0.386±0.036 vs EEG-only re-centered Riemann 0.580 (Δ -0.194, fair null). Old wrong-quadrant number was 0.34-0.40 -> sign fix barely moved it. CONCLUSION: the ERD anti-correlation sign was NOT why output-space fusion fails; the MULTIPLICATIVE joint (clean EEG × weak fNIRS × coverage, drops phase/envelope) is architecturally lossy. Confirms the standing conclusion: output-space fusion adds nothing decodable; source-space/generative fusion (Stage-4, wv1 epic) is the real untested lever. 060 = correctness improvement + negative confirmation, not a decode win. Operator kept physically-correct + documented.

Domain-adversarial (bd 36g) FINAL verdict — NOT a headline lever for Stage-3 EEG->image cross-subject transfer. train4/test5 25ep. Constant w0.5 lambda1.0, 3 seeds: single-trial top1 base 1.87±0.14 vs adv 2.00±0.06 (Δ+0.13pp, WITHIN noise — single-seed +0.32 washed out); single-trial top5 base 7.75±0.17 vs adv 8.18±0.17 (Δ+0.43 ~2.5sigma, weak REAL but ranking-only); concept-avg nil. lambda-RAMP (0->1 sched, single seed): single1 1.89 / single5 7.78 = AT BASELINE, worse than constant adv — ramp does NOT rescue. Adversary acts (consistent val-down ~4.7 vs 5.2) but benefit is a marginal top5 nudge, never reaches single-trial-top1. CONCLUSION: neither input-alignment (dpi, killed) nor embedding-adversarial (36g, weak-top5-only) closes the cross-subject single-trial-top1 gap at train-4. The gap is a genuine hard ceiling for this compact encoder at this scale — subject-invariance is NOT the missing piece; likely single-trial SNR / encoder capacity. Reaffirmed: trust multi-seed over single-seed on near-chance retrieval.

Per-subject raw-signal re-centering (recenter_signals, W=M^-1/2 whitening) is NEUTRAL on the honest metric, not a clear fix — Stage-3 EEG->image, matched train4/test5 25ep: single-trial top1 UNCHANGED (diff -0.00006, noise), concept-avg WORSE (-0.025). DIAGNOSED (ruled out bug + bias): whitening output finite/O(1) std 1.7 = no numerical bug; test concepts interleaved 80-84 reps each so strided-512 M-sample is representative = no data bias. REAL cause = NOISE AMPLIFICATION: test-subject M cond=1491 (min eig 6.6e-3), full whitening equalizes ALL directions incl noise channels (~38x relative boost), injecting noise -> raises within-concept embedding dispersion -> concept-AVG (averages 80 reps) tanks while single-trial (per-rep, already noisy) stays neutral. NOT a fundamental failure of the alignment idea — the naive full-whitening conflates alignment BENEFIT with noise COST (net neutral single-trial). Fix to test: SHRINKAGE-regularized whitening (align dominant directions only, don't amplify noise floor) — could tip single-trial positive. Earlier residual-rotation hypothesis unconfirmed/secondary; noise-amplification is the measured cause. Code opt-in (TrainConfig.recenter default False).

fNIRS-informed weighted EEG inverse (bd 4so, Liu-1998 fMRI-informed prior K=R Gᵀ(GRGᵀ+λ²I)⁻¹ with R=diag(fNIRS activation)) does NOT beat a plain inverse for cross-subject source-space workload decode — WIRED + RAN (source_prior_decode.py, Shin n-back paired EEG+fNIRS, 702 blocks/26 subj, per-subject unsupervised fNIRS HbO-magnitude prior RBF-interpolated onto fsaverage sources, 68-DK parcels, re-centered tangent). Four matched arms all within fold noise (std~0.08, SE~0.036): sensor 0.581, dSPM 0.592, uniform-prior 0.589, fNIRS-prior 0.593. Δ fNIRS−uniform +0.003, Δ fNIRS−dSPM +0.001 = clear null. The fNIRS 'where' regularizes the ill-posed inverse but does NOT inform the workload discriminant. Confirms source-space-decode-null (728) extends to the fNIRS-priored inverse: EEG+fNIRS complementarity is not decode-accessible in output space (060), plain source space (728), OR fNIRS-informed source space (4so). Operator correct + now consumed.

Frozen CBraMod perception head-search (nm5/j4l, cross-subject train[1-4]->test5, S=1/200Hz, 120ep, matched cached features). Single-trial top1 / concept top1: mean_lin 0.60/0.50 (chance floor, identical at 60+120ep) < flat_mlp 1.21/3.00 (unordered bag of 63 channel-tokens) < pos_attn 1.59/4.00 (electrode position-embedding + self-attention) < topo_cnn 1.68/4.50 (RBF-interp onto scalp grid + 2D CNN, Bashivan 2016). GEOMETRY WINS MONOTONICALLY. Two findings: (1) SPATIAL PRESERVATION is the lever not head depth/pooling (mean/attn pool=chance, keeping tokens doubles it); (2) folding by ELECTRODE GEOMETRY beats the unordered bag and the best frozen head (topo_cnn 1.68%) BEATS from-scratch NICE (1.6%) with ZERO backbone training. All geometry heads still undertrained (val climbing at ep118/120) = lower bounds. Confirms owner's intuition: head engineering on frozen features > backbone adaptation. PR #44. Positions from eeg_positions(channels()) standard_1005 unit-disk. Next (j4l): longer schedule (undertrained), GCN arm, tune grid/sigma, then LOSO.

CORRECTED 2026-07-16: MVNN is NOT the perception default — z-score is. The b40j promotion rested on a +0.35pp win that was a TRANSDUCTIVE LEAK (whitener fit on the scored test trials using their image labels = the retrieval target). Leak-free per-subject MVNN (each subject's Sigma^-1/2 fit on its OWN training-image trials, disjoint from the 200 scored test images, applied to that subject) scores single-top1 1.66 vs z-score 1.60 = +0.06, WITHIN single-seed noise. TrainConfig.normalize default = 'auto' = z-score for every encoder. MVNN kept as a physically-correct, leak-free --normalize override (mild ranking edge top5 7.46/rank 68/margin 0.019, concept-avg COST 14.5<18), never the default. See interpretations/visual/2026-07-16_mvnn_per_subject_calibration.md.

CBraMod foundation encoder on Stage-3 cross-subject perception (epic bji, matched trainer, resample200, perception_converged 60ep, chance 0.5%): frozen < NICE < FINE-TUNED, REPLICATED on 2 held-out subjects. Per-subject single-trial top1 [test5 / test1]: CBraMod-FROZEN 0.63 (probe, ~chance) < NICE-from-scratch 1.60/1.66 < CBraMod-FINE-TUNED (unfrozen, lr1e-4) 2.38/2.04. Fine-tuned beats NICE on ALL metrics BOTH subjects (8/8): single-top5 9.58/8.71 vs 7.05/7.59, concept-top1 4.5/6.0 vs 3.0/5.5, concept-top5 20.0/19.0 vs 18.0/16.5, val 7.51/6.96 vs 5.43/5.03. Mean single-top1 ~2.21 vs ~1.63 = ~1.35x NICE, consistent direction. Both ft runs UNDER-trained (best ep59/51, val still climbing) -> lower bound. VERDICT (confirmed): pretrained capacity ADAPTED is a REAL lever; the cross-subject perception wall is NOT a pure single-trial SNR floor. First method in the arc to beat the ~1.9% NICE baseline (dpi/36g/lbd all null). Frozen linear-probe FAILS (clinical TUEG features don't encode perception discriminants) — only fine-tuning cashes it. CAVEATS: 2 subjects not full 10-LOSO, single seed each, one untuned lr (backbone=head). Hardening = follow-up (LOSO multi-seed, longer schedule, discriminative-lr/LoRA, spatial head).

If a result looks too good vs SOTA/published, assume a bug/leak/eval-mismatch, not a breakthrough — verify under matched eval before claiming. Origin: fNIRS 0.442 'beat' BenchNIRS 0.389 was apples-to-oranges (LOSO+shrinkage vs 5-fold-group+plain-LDA); real matched result was a modest +3.7pp (0.429 vs reproduced 0.392).

SUPERSEDED 2026-07-18: the sklearn-estimator face for braindecode nets (kvb) was built then REMOVED same-day - it was DEAD CODE (zero consumers: no cross_validate/GridSearch/Pipeline uses it) and adds surface without reducing anything. Lesson: sklearn-compat here is speculative (fold-parallel env-dead 07n; no calibration/GridSearch need queued) - don't build an interoperability face with no consumer. The trainer work was REDIRECTED to the real consolidation (bd 1eca, DONE): extract neuroscan/training.py = TorchPerf.enable_fast_matmul (was VERBATIM-dup in decoders.py + train_nice.py) + EarlyStopper (mode max=val-top1 / min=val-loss via sign-normalized compare + min_delta; best-ckpt cpu-clone + patience + restore). Both trainers call one home, behaviour-preserving (11 tests), net -73 lines. This is the n=2 REAL cross-paradigm scaffold consolidation. 3cy (contrastive loss/data seam merge) STILL deferred - gated on a 2nd contrastive method, NOT a backbone swap; seam in 3cy bead design (standalone md was removed as premature). PRINCIPLE reaffirmed: ideal PR adds no functionality, reduces/reorganizes; dead code with no caller is the anti-pattern even if unit-tested.

Continuous perception eval metrics (bd 2y7k, Nice.retrieval_continuous): cos_to_true, margin (cos_true - mean cos-to-others), mean_rank, cos_to_true_z (sigma above the candidate bank's own off-diagonal cos = random-concept-pair baseline). Extras alongside top-k accuracy, never replacing it. KEY FACTS from frozen CBraMod head zoo backfill (train1-4/test5, 120ep off cache): (1) two random THINGS concepts sit at cos 0.334+-0.092 in CLIP space (NOT ~0 - CLIP concepts cluster tightly, pairs up to 0.80), so absolute cos-to-true is not self-interpretable. (2) EVERY arm points ~ORTHOGONAL to true concept: cos-to-true 0.001-0.058 = arccos ~86-90deg, incl the best (topo 1.75%). Model never lands NEAR the right concept absolutely. (3) All z NEGATIVE (-3.0 to -3.6): EEG embedding is FARTHER from its true concept than two random concepts are from each other - because EEG embeddings sit OUTSIDE the tight CLIP concept cluster (fixed geometry offset, not arm quality). (4) raw cos/z are GEOMETRY-CONFOUNDED; what tracks accuracy is MARGIN (0.001 mean_lin -> 0.017 pos_attn) and MEAN_RANK (97->70, ~monotone with top1). InfoNCE optimizes relative closeness so the meaningful continuous metric is relative. (5) retrieval lives in a TINY positive margin (topo 1.75%=3.5x chance from margin 0.015) - single-trial cross-subject signal is a faint relative tilt, not a semantic hit. (6) higher accuracy does NOT = tighter angular error (cos_std ~0.02-0.03 flat across arms). Full table: mean_lin 0.60/gcn 1.07/flat 1.21/pos_attn 1.59/topo 1.75 single-top1. Interpretation writeup interpretations/visual/2026-07-14_perception_continuous_metrics.md. CAVEAT: single-trial embeddings; trial-averaged (concept_avg, higher acc) may align better - untested continuous cut. Metric mirrors loss: val_cos_to_true tracks val_top1 (NICE smoke 0.062->0.084 as top1 1.58->2.55).

pyriemann 0.11/0.12.dev = pure numpy (grepped installed source + docs + web: zero torch/array-api refs, deps numpy/scipy/sklearn/joblib). No GPU path without from-scratch torch reimpl of the geometry ops. Bench (numpy, BLAS-threaded): mean_riemann 22x22=36ms, 28x28=396ms, 72x72=5.9s for ~200 mats. Our riemann runs are 22-28 dim (bnci MI, eeg n-back) => already 36-400ms/fold, NOT a bottleneck; the 72-dim fnirs path uses fnirs_lda (features->LDA), never mean_riemann. Verdict: GPU-via-pyriemann = no win (no path + not the bottleneck + transfer overhead would dominate at these sizes). Deferred, do not wire.

Stage-4 fusion (wv1) foundation built. (1) 060: output-space EEG+fNIRS fusion (multiplicative sensor joint) re-measured = fair null (0.386 vs EEG-only 0.580) even after the ERD sign fix -> output-space is NOT the lever. (2) 728: built the EEG->cortex source operator (core/features/eeg/source.py) — fsaverage TEMPLATE forward (lead field, no individual MRI) + dSPM minimum-norm inverse -> 68-parcel Desikan-Killiany source series [n,68,t], disk-cached per montage (instant rebuild), verified end-to-end. Needs nibabel ([source] extra) + MNE fsaverage (present at D:/data). This is the substrate for SOURCE-space fusion: co-register EEG cortical estimate with fNIRS optical sources instead of multiplying sensor mixtures. REMAINING wv1: 7jn (external mesh-MonteCarlo fNIRS simulator, arXiv 2605.30552 — needs repo checkout under external/), uqw (validate coupling/CBSI vs that sim — gated on 7jn, self-validation is circular), 4so (fNIRS spatial prior on the EEG inverse — builds on 728). Source-space fusion decode integration not yet run.

Source-space EEG projection does NOT beat sensor space for cross-subject decode (bd 728, workload n-back). Ran source_decode.py: sensor (28ch) re-centered-tangent cross-subject acc 0.594±0.060 vs source (68 Desikan-Killiany parcels via fsaverage template forward + dSPM inverse) 0.602±0.063, Δ+0.008 = FAIR NULL. Template-head source localization adds regularization, not information — the sensor covariance already captures the cross-subject-transferable structure. IMPORTANT: this REFUTES the wv1 Phase-2 gate claim 'source-space gives a lever output-space lacked' — that was asserted from the 060 output-space null + the operators existing, WITHOUT running the decode. Actually run = null. Caveats (untested): individual-MRI (vs template) head models, a different decoder, or the fNIRS-prior weighted inverse (4so, unrun) could still differ. But plain source projection = no decode lever. The 728 operator is correct + now USED (source_decode), just not a decode win. Lesson: don't close a gate on 'operator built'; run the decode.

CBraMod backbone is HARD-WIRED to 200Hz / 200-point patches = 1.0s per patch, with an rfft SPECTRAL embedding (101 bins) baked into pretrained patch-embedding weights (verified external/CBraMod: tuab reshape(16,10,200) on 10s epochs, bciciv2a 22x4x200 on 4s trials). For THINGS-EEG2 (1s stimulus): S = T//200 = 1, so the whole trial is ONE patch and fine ERP timing is COMPRESSED inside the frozen patch rep, unreachable by any head. CANNOT get S>1 by resampling up (400/800Hz -> each 200pt patch becomes 0.5/0.25s -> spectral bins map to wrong physical freqs -> corrupts the pretrained prior) NOR by smaller patches (proj_in trained for 200pts). At resample=200 we are CORRECT/in-distribution. Sub-patch temporal signal reachable ONLY by weight adaptation: full fine-tune=2.38% (proven) or LoRA (cheap, bd 29z). Task is image reconstruction so coarse temporal may be acceptable.

CBraMod pretraining-faithful input scale (microvolts/100, the amplitude-preserving Scale link) does NOT help the FROZEN CBraMod probe — REFUTES the pfad/jwzl hypothesis at the frozen stage (bd 7mi4). Primary source: CBraMod pretrain_trainer.py:64 does x/100 (NOT z-score; the deep-dive + foundation docstring claim of z-score was WRONG). Our raw is Volts; Scale(1e4) = V x1e6 (uV) /100 = pretraining range (p99~0.83, verified). Matched frozen head search train[1-4]/test5 60ep: Scale vs z-score single-trial top1 — mean_lin 0.58/0.60, flat 1.27/1.21, pos_attn 1.23/1.59, topo 1.21/1.75, gcn 0.99/1.07. Scale specifically KILLS the geometry heads (topo -0.54, pos_attn -0.36); flat (geometry-blind) unaffected. MECHANISM: z-score equalizes per-channel variance so geometry heads read scalp spatial structure; amplitude-preserving scale lets high-variance channels dominate, washing out spatial signal (geometry collapses to flat under scale). CBraMod auto-chain = z-score (evidenced default + all existing ft/LoRA used it). CAVEAT: frozen probe can't adapt backbone to exploit correct-scale input; whether scale helps FINE-TUNING is the open axis (follow-up m3mi, --normalize scale override wired). Chain infra (bd 4aoz, core/normalization/) stands regardless.

CORRECTED 2026-07-16: MVNN does NOT beat z-score for Stage-3 cross-subject single-trial retrieval — the b40j +0.35pp was a TRANSDUCTIVE LEAK. b40j fit the per-subject whitener on the SCORED test trials using their condition=image labels; image identity IS the retrieval target -> leak. Leak-free redo: per-subject whitener fit on each subject's own TRAINING-image trials (disjoint from scored test images), applied per-subject (unbatched-real: enroll once, matmul per trial). Matched perception_converged train[1-4]/test5 single-seed: clean per-subject MVNN single-top1 1.66 vs z-score 1.60 (+0.06 = noise), single-top5 7.46 vs 7.05, mean_rank 68 vs 71, margin 0.019 vs 0.016, concept-top5 14.5 vs 18 (WORSE — whitening distorts concept-avg, per-rep denoising vanishes over 80 reps). A WRONG global-pooled-whitener impl scored 1.44 (don't cite as 'MVNN loses' — that cripples the per-subject method). Net: leak-free MVNN ~= z-score, mild ranking edge + concept-avg cost, NOT a lever. z-score stays default. Cross-subject wall lever is encoder capacity/adaptation not input norm (perception-input-align-not-bottleneck).

Stage-3 EEG->image cross-subject single-trial retrieval sits in a tight ~1.7-1.9% band REGARDLESS of per-subject input alignment (baseline 1.71, full-recenter 1.71, shrinkage-0.1 1.86 — all train4/test5 25ep, single-seed so +/-0.15pp = noise). Tested: no-align, full covariance whitening, shrinkage-regularized whitening (cond 1491->61). None convincingly beats baseline single-trial; full/shrink recenter HURT concept-avg (noise amplification, see perception-signal-recenter-fails). CONCLUSION: input-space per-subject alignment is NOT the cross-subject bottleneck for this learned encoder (unlike MI/workload where it was, feeding classical classifiers). The residual whitening removes isn't what breaks transfer. Next levers target the ENCODER learning a subject-invariant SEMANTIC map: (a) domain-adversarial gradient-reversal on subject id (bd 36g, embedding-space invariance); (b) bigger encoder / longer training (current 693k, early-stops ~ep9-15). recenter code kept opt-in+documented, dig complete.

7tl retrieval-trainer efficiency epic RAN (train1-4/test5, 40ep, resample200, bf16, single-seed, vitb32). THREE deliverables all land, NONE a top-k gain (as forecast). (1) SPEED WIN: bf16 13.8s/ep vs fp32 ~43s =3.1x; +per-epoch subsample train_frac0.2 =2.8s/ep =~15x fp32 at IDENTICAL single-top1 (1.82=1.82, MRR/median-rank/PR-AUC if anything a hair better). NICE 0.69M net over-fed at 240k trials/ep so 1/5 subset costs nothing = free speedup, parity holds. (2) METRICS: wired Retrieval.retrieval_metrics (MRR/median-rank/PR-AUC/recall@k) into primary cross-subject evaluate() [N,C] score matrix - was cross_dataset-only; true concept sits at MEDIAN RANK ~55/200 = faint-tilt floor made legible. Permanent honest-reporting value. (3) NEGATIVE-QUALITY ABLATION = clean KILL: isolated sampling (train_frac1.0+bf16 fixed) uniform 1.82 -> balanced(ewd) 1.77 (-0.05) -> clip_hard(mnr, hard_beta0) 1.71 (-0.11), MONOTONE worse across single-top1/MRR/median-rank/PR-AUC, all within +-0.14 noise, both ~1.5x slower (21s vs 14s). lbd precedent (soft-neg washed 1.73v1.87) now confirmed on batch-CONSTRUCTION axis: ~86-90deg off-concept = faint relative tilt not separable margin, so harder/balanced negatives sharpen a boundary the per-trial SNR cant populate; clip_hard MOST negative (confusable concepts every batch = gradient noise). VERDICT: keep subsample+bf16 ON (free), keep rank-metrics (permanent), KILL balanced/clip_hard as defaults (opt-in, washed+slower), no multiseed warranted. Cross-subject wall is capacity/SNR not negative-selection - confirmed on a 4th axis. Interp interpretations/visual/2026-07-18_retrieval_trainer_speed_and_negative_ablation.md

At the FINDING stage (no working number yet), skip sweeps/ablations/multi-seed/k-fold — they are PERFECTING tools that harden an already-working result, and at finding-stage they cost huge wall-time for ~zero signal about whether a method is better. Multi-seed parity / subject-count sweeps on a near-chance result teach nothing. Right early loop: method tweak -> CHEAPEST test that shows a signal (single run, small data, fast) -> keep or kill -> next tweak. Precision AFTER a number moves. Owner's constraint is TIME-TO-DELIVER; do not spend it measuring a broken method harder. Applies across all personal_projects. Origin: I mis-set Stage-3 perception Phase-1 goal to a subject-count 'headline' sweep + multi-seed parity when cross-subject retrieval was still near-chance (~1.9%); owner correctly called it premature rigor. No SOTA paper (e.g. NICE) leads with subject-count — they chase the retrieval number via method/representation.

Per-subject z-scoring is a real cross-subject transfer fix for EEG WORKLOAD band-power (the EEG analog of Riemannian re-centering for MI). Band-power is subject-idiosyncratic in ABSOLUTE scale; z-scoring each subject by its own feature mean/std (unsupervised, no labels) lifts EEG-bandpower->LDA from 0.407 raw to 0.511 (honest calibration-half) / 0.581 (transductive, uses test blocks' own stats). fNIRS does NOT benefit (0.474->0.448/0.483) - already per-epoch baseline-corrected so subject offset removed. So earlier 'EEG near-floor cross-subject regardless of feature' was WRONG - it was missing per-subject normalization. With it EEG (~0.51-0.58) >= fNIRS (~0.47), EEG is the stronger workload modality. IMPORTANT: transductive z (test-block stats) is ~7pt optimistic vs honest calib-half; report the calib-half ~0.51 as the defensible number.

TF32 (torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high') + allow_tf32 matmul/cudnn) is the ONE cheap parity-safe training speedup for mindscape on the 5090 — MEASURED -22% step time (bd 62ak microbench, NICE encoder B512 C63 T250): baseline 37.1ms/step -> 29.0ms, the win entirely in BACKWARD (25.8->17.3ms; backward = 70% of step). Parity-safe: training already runs bf16 autocast, TF32's 10-bit mantissa is MORE precise than the bf16 already in use, so no matched arm needed. Landed in both trainers: TrainNice._enable_fast_matmul (called in _build_optim, every perception path) + BraindecodeClf._enable_fast_matmul (classical/motor-imagery fit). DEAD levers (measured/ruled out, do not retry): (1) cudnn.benchmark = NEUTRAL/worse (38.1ms) for the small NICE convs + variable end-of-epoch batch shapes re-trigger its autotune + cu130 batch>=2048 illegal-access caveat; (2) torch.compile = UNAVAILABLE on Windows (no Triton wheel -> TritonMissing), platform dead-end; (3) GPU-resident fit-set / prefetch: data path (gather 4.1 + h2d 1.8 = 5.9ms) is only 16% of step for this backward-bound net, so the big-effort GPU-resident rewrite buys <=16% and needs a VRAM gate + parity run -> low priority, deferred to follow-up.

Geometry-aware spatial prior on the TRAINABLE NICE encoder (bd 1x0, graph-Laplacian smoothness penalty tr(WtLW) on the spatial conv, matched train[1-4]/test5 seed0 60ep σ0.2, λ0/0.005/0.02): single-top1 1.53->1.65->1.67 (+0.14 at λ0.02 = single-seed noise band ±0.14, forecast to WASH under multi-seed per the 36g precedent where single-seed +0.32 washed to +0.13±0.06). The REAL and coherent effect is REGULARIZATION not retrieval: at λ0.02 margin +25% (0.0167->0.0209), mean_rank -1.9 (70.2->68.3), best-val ep19 vs baseline ep47 (early-stopped ~ep31, matched/beat baseline in 2/3 fewer epochs, no overfit). VERDICT: physically-sound mild REGULARIZER, NOT a headline floor-breaker — a trainable conv already re-learns channel adjacency from data so imposing it is redundant on volume-conduction-smeared EEG (confirms 1x0's own fNIRS-first physics caveat: fNIRS channels are genuinely local so adjacency is real info there, unlike EEG). KEEP opt-in+documented (TrainConfig.geo_lambda/geo_sigma off default); KILL as a perception headline lever; do NOT multi-seed on perception (forecast to wash). Redirect 1x0 to fNIRS n-back. Perception floor stays capacity/SNR (ft 2.38/LoRA 2.10 > NICE 1.60), not a structure-prior problem. Reusable substrate EegMontage.channel_laplacian + NiceEncoder.geo_penalty stands.

Domain-randomization augmentation (bd jdh, domain_randomize: log-normal coupling gain + common-mode systemic + timing jitter) is NOT a cross-subject lever for fNIRS n-back — WIRED + RAN (augment_probe.py, 0-back-vs-2-back binary, 468 epochs/26 subj, 5-fold). Two findings: (1) NO cross-subject headroom — within-subject 0.690 vs cross-subject 0.679 = +0.011 gap, so the fNIRS workload contrast already transfers, nothing for augmentation to fix; (2) augmentation HURTS -0.109 (cross+aug 0.570 vs plain 0.679) because FnirsLda reads amplitude features (mean/slope/peak) and gain+systemic+shift perturb exactly those. Mechanism shipped + honestly falsified as a decode lever on the available task (premise 'nuisance-driven cross-subject loss' doesn't hold for Shin n-back). jdh's acceptance ('gain on a task with headroom') is unmeetable with our fNIRS data — no such task exists here.
